address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県三木市志染町御坂243', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Misaka-jinja (Miki)}} [[JA:御坂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11488875}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|兵庫県三木市の神社|周防国の式内社|三坂神社 (山口市)}} {{神社 |名称 = 御坂神社 |画像 = [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-3.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|三木市|Q31238}}志染町御坂243 |位置 = {{Coord|34|47|29.1|N|135|03|23.9|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 八戸桂須御諸神 |社格 = {{jalink|式内小社|Q134917287}}・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = 三間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}銅板葺 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 5月3日 |神事 = 御弓祭（2月11日）・御田祭（おんださい）（5月5日） |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御坂神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''御坂神社'''（みさかじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|三木市|Q31238}}志染町御坂243に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に「{{jalink|播磨国|Q1047523}}{{jalink|美嚢郡|Q6868901|美嚢（みのう）郡}}一座 御坂神社」と記載された{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。志染の{{jalink|氏神|Q767655}}。 === 祭神 === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-5.jpg|thumb|right|260px|拝殿から本殿を見る]] '''八戸桂掛須御諸神'''（やとかけすみもろのかみ）を主祭神に{{jalink|大物主神}}と{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|葦原志男神}}を配祀するが、3神とも{{jalink|大国主神}}の別名である[https://misaka-jinja.com/gosaijin/ 御坂神社公式HP-御坂社（御坂神社）の御祭神]。 === 歴史 === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-Honden.jpg|thumb|right|260px|拝殿と本殿]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Nou Butai.jpg|thumb|right|260px|{{jalink|能舞台}}]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-1.jpg|thumb|right|260px|御坂神社の杜。陰陽石が祀られる。]] 鎮座年代は不明。『{{jalink|播磨国風土記|Q11498726}}』には「志染の里三坂にます神八戸掛須御諸神は、大物主と葦原志男の国堅めたまひし以後に天より三坂峯に下り給ひき」との記載があり、第17代天皇、{{jalink|履中天皇|Q329704}}が参拝になったとも伝えられる。その際に、履中天皇が、「この川の流れは大変美しい」といったことにちなみ、この地域一体を「美嚢郡」と呼ぶようになったという。また、天皇の食膳に{{jalink|シジミ|Q1796597}}が這いあがったことから、この地をしじみ（志染）というようになったという[https://misaka-jinja.com/yuisyo/ 御坂神社公式HP-御坂社（御坂神社）の由緒] 。 {{jalink|天正|Q1145058|天正年間}}、{{jalink|豊臣秀吉|Q187550|羽柴秀吉}}が中国討伐の際、その途上に三木の{{jalink|別所長治|Q837435}}の居城{{jalink|三木城|Q11242473|釜山}}攻めを行ったが、そのときの兵火（{{jalink|天正の兵乱}}）により古い記録がすべて焼失したため、詳細が不明になった。その後、{{jalink|慶長|Q1328185}}13年（{{jalink|1608年|Q6758}}）現在地へ社地を移したと伝えられる。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に郷社に列し、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}3年（{{jalink|1914年|Q2083}}）4月{{jalink|神饌幣帛料供進神社}}の指定を受けた。 なお、三木市内には「'''みさか'''」と呼ばれる神社が当御坂神社のほか、'''三坂神社'''、'''美坂神社'''、'''御酒神社'''など計8ヵ所ある。 === 境内 === 本殿ならびに本殿に構造的につながった拝殿とそれらの正面に能舞台と社務所を置き、本殿裏側は杜になっており、陰陽石が祀られる[https://misaka-jinja.com/gallery/ 御坂神社公式HP-Photo]。 === 祭礼日 [https://misaka-jinja.com/event/ 御坂神社公式HP-年間行事予定]=== *{{jalink|1月1日|Q2150}} {{jalink|歳旦祭|Q11546824}} *{{jalink|2月11日|Q2335}} 御弓祭 以前は、{{jalink|旧正月|Q1944136}}初亥の日に行われていたものの、{{jalink|昭和55年}}より{{jalink|建国記念日|Q12981823}}である2月11日に変更された。6名の射手により1回に2本ずつ5回で計10本の{{jalink|矢|Q45922}}が約20m 離れた{{jalink|的|Q11269217}}をめがけて放たれ、的の中心に当たるとその年は{{jalink|五穀豊穣}}となり、的の裏側に封じ込めた「鬼」というところに当たった場合は「[[Wikt:破邪|破邪]]顕正」（はじゃけんしょう）といわれ、邪気をくじき、正しい道理を世間に現し流布するとされる。本来は射手は神社脇の{{jalink|志染川|Q11491046}}にて{{jalink|禊|Q1936265}}を行った後、拝殿で無言で{{jalink|神酒|Q3313722}}を受ける「長床の儀」を行っていたが現在は省略されている。その他、{{jalink|巫女|Q1047139}}による「浦安の舞い」、{{jalink|猿田彦}}による「天地祓い、四方祓い」が奉納され、最後に{{jalink|餅まき|Q11666809}}が行われる。 *{{jalink|2月11日|Q2335}} 祈年祭 *5月4,5日 例祭 2台の{{jalink|屋台|Q2039612}}が奉納される。 *5月例祭後の日曜日 {{jalink|御田祭}} *{{jalink|6月30日|Q2657}} {{jalink|大祓|Q11261910|大祓祭}} *{{jalink|11月23日|Q3021}} {{jalink|新嘗祭|Q11501518}} *{{jalink|12月28日|Q2832}} 大祓祭 === ギャラリー === File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-1.jpg|{{jalink|初詣|Q1032381}}風景 2015年度 File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-6.jpg|{{jalink|拝殿|Q3125745}}から{{jalink|能舞台}}方向を見る File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-2.jpg|{{jalink|陰陽石}}({{jalink|男性|Q8441}}） File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-3.jpg|陰陽石({{jalink|女性|Q467}}） === 参考文献 === *『式内社調査報告』第22巻山陽道、皇學館大學出版部、昭和55年 === 交通 === *自動車：{{jalink|山陽自動車道|Q7413195}}{{jalink|三木東インターチェンジ|Q10866141}}下車、約5分。 === 周辺 === *{{jalink|御坂サイフォン橋|Q11488870}} *{{jalink|志染の石室|Q11491045}} === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === {{Commons|Misaka-jinja (Miki)}} *[https://misaka-jinja.com/ 御坂神社]（神社公式） *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6310024.html 御阪社]（兵庫県神社庁） *[http://www.kita-harima.jp/modules/xdirectory/singlelink.php?cid=110&lid=220 御坂神社]（三木市観光協会） *[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/video/bura1708.htm 風土記の里 志染]（三木市インターネットビデオ放送局「ぶらりと三木へ」） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みさかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:三木市の建築物]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{jalink|天正|Q1145058|天正年間}}、{{jalink|豊臣秀吉|Q187550|羽柴秀吉}}が中国討伐の際、その途上に三木の{{jalink|別所長治|Q837435}}の居城{{jalink|三木城|Q11242473|釜山}}攻めを行ったが、そのときの兵火（{{jalink|天正の兵乱}}）により古い記録がすべて焼失したため、詳細が不明になった。その後、{{jalink|慶長|Q1328185}}13年（{{jalink|1608年|Q6758}}）現在地へ社地を移したと伝えられる。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に郷社に列し、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}3年（{{jalink|1914年|Q2083}}）4月{{jalink|神饌幣帛料供進神社}}の指定を受けた。 なお、三木市内には「'''みさか'''」と呼ばれる神社が当御坂神社のほか、'''三坂神社'''、'''美坂神社'''、'''御酒神社'''など計8ヵ所ある。 === 境内 === 本殿ならびに本殿に構造的につながった拝殿とそれらの正面に能舞台と社務所を置き、本殿裏側は杜になっており、陰陽石が祀られる[https://misaka-jinja.com/gallery/ 御坂神社公式HP-Photo]。 === 祭礼日 [https://misaka-jinja.com/event/ 御坂神社公式HP-年間行事予定]=== *{{jalink|1月1日|Q2150}} {{jalink|歳旦祭|Q11546824}} *{{jalink|2月11日|Q2335}} 御弓祭 以前は、{{jalink|旧正月|Q1944136}}初亥の日に行われていたものの、{{jalink|昭和55年}}より{{jalink|建国記念日|Q12981823}}である2月11日に変更された。6名の射手により1回に2本ずつ5回で計10本の{{jalink|矢|Q45922}}が約20m 離れた{{jalink|的|Q11269217}}をめがけて放たれ、的の中心に当たるとその年は{{jalink|五穀豊穣}}となり、的の裏側に封じ込めた「鬼」というところに当たった場合は「[[Wikt:破邪|破邪]]顕正」（はじゃけんしょう）といわれ、邪気をくじき、正しい道理を世間に現し流布するとされる。本来は射手は神社脇の{{jalink|志染川|Q11491046}}にて{{jalink|禊|Q1936265}}を行った後、拝殿で無言で{{jalink|神酒|Q3313722}}を受ける「長床の儀」を行っていたが現在は省略されている。その他、{{jalink|巫女|Q1047139}}による「浦安の舞い」、{{jalink|猿田彦}}による「天地祓い、四方祓い」が奉納され、最後に{{jalink|餅まき|Q11666809}}が行われる。 *{{jalink|2月11日|Q2335}} 祈年祭 *5月4,5日 例祭 2台の{{jalink|屋台|Q2039612}}が奉納される。 *5月例祭後の日曜日 {{jalink|御田祭}} *{{jalink|6月30日|Q2657}} {{jalink|大祓|Q11261910|大祓祭}} *{{jalink|11月23日|Q3021}} {{jalink|新嘗祭|Q11501518}} *{{jalink|12月28日|Q2832}} 大祓祭 === ギャラリー === File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-1.jpg|{{jalink|初詣|Q1032381}}風景 2015年度 File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-6.jpg|{{jalink|拝殿|Q3125745}}から{{jalink|能舞台}}方向を見る File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-2.jpg|{{jalink|陰陽石}}({{jalink|男性|Q8441}}） File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-3.jpg|陰陽石({{jalink|女性|Q467}}） === 参考文献 === *『式内社調査報告』第22巻山陽道、皇學館大學出版部、昭和55年 === 交通 === *自動車：{{jalink|山陽自動車道|Q7413195}}{{jalink|三木東インターチェンジ|Q10866141}}下車、約5分。 === 周辺 === *{{jalink|御坂サイフォン橋|Q11488870}} *{{jalink|志染の石室|Q11491045}} === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === {{Commons|Misaka-jinja (Miki)}} *[https://misaka-jinja.com/ 御坂神社]（神社公式） *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6310024.html 御阪社]（兵庫県神社庁） *[http://www.kita-harima.jp/modules/xdirectory/singlelink.php?cid=110&lid=220 御坂神社]（三木市観光協会） *[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/video/bura1708.htm 風土記の里 志染]（三木市インターネットビデオ放送局「ぶらりと三木へ」） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みさかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated {{Otheruses|兵庫県三木市の神社|周防国の式内社|三坂神社 (山口市)}} {{神社 |名称 = 御坂神社 |画像 = [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-3.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|三木市|Q31238}}志染町御坂243 |位置 = {{Coord|34|47|29.1|N|135|03|23.9|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 八戸桂須御諸神 |社格 = {{jalink|式内小社|Q134917287}}・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = 三間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}銅板葺 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 5月3日 |神事 = 御弓祭（2月11日）・御田祭（おんださい）（5月5日） |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御坂神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''御坂神社'''（みさかじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|三木市|Q31238}}志染町御坂243に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に「{{jalink|播磨国|Q1047523}}{{jalink|美嚢郡|Q6868901|美嚢（みのう）郡}}一座 御坂神社」と記載された{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。志染の{{jalink|氏神|Q767655}}。 === 祭神 === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-5.jpg|thumb|right|260px|拝殿から本殿を見る]] '''八戸桂掛須御諸神'''（やとかけすみもろのかみ）を主祭神に{{jalink|大物主神}}と{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|葦原志男神}}を配祀するが、3神とも{{jalink|大国主神}}の別名である[https://misaka-jinja.com/gosaijin/ 御坂神社公式HP-御坂社（御坂神社）の御祭神]。 === 歴史 === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-Honden.jpg|thumb|right|260px|拝殿と本殿]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Nou Butai.jpg|thumb|right|260px|{{jalink|能舞台}}]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-1.jpg|thumb|right|260px|御坂神社の杜。陰陽石が祀られる。]] 鎮座年代は不明。『{{jalink|播磨国風土記|Q11498726}}』には「志染の里三坂にます神八戸掛須御諸神は、大物主と葦原志男の国堅めたまひし以後に天より三坂峯に下り給ひき」との記載があり、第17代天皇、{{jalink|履中天皇|Q329704}}が参拝になったとも伝えられる。その際に、履中天皇が、「この川の流れは大変美しい」といったことにちなみ、この地域一体を「美嚢郡」と呼ぶようになったという。また、天皇の食膳に{{jalink|シジミ|Q1796597}}が這いあがったことから、この地をしじみ（志染）というようになったという[https://misaka-jinja.com/yuisyo/ 御坂神社公式HP-御坂社（御坂神社）の由緒] 。 {{jalink|天正|Q1145058|天正年間}}、{{jalink|豊臣秀吉|Q187550|羽柴秀吉}}が中国討伐の際、その途上に三木の{{jalink|別所長治|Q837435}}の居城{{jalink|三木城|Q11242473|釜山}}攻めを行ったが、そのときの兵火（{{jalink|天正の兵乱}}）により古い記録がすべて焼失したため、詳細が不明になった。その後、{{jalink|慶長|Q1328185}}13年（{{jalink|1608年|Q6758}}）現在地へ社地を移したと伝えられる。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に郷社に列し、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}3年（{{jalink|1914年|Q2083}}）4月{{jalink|神饌幣帛料供進神社}}の指定を受けた。 なお、三木市内には「'''みさか'''」と呼ばれる神社が当御坂神社のほか、'''三坂神社'''、'''美坂神社'''、'''御酒神社'''など計8ヵ所ある。 === 境内 === 本殿ならびに本殿に構造的につながった拝殿とそれらの正面に能舞台と社務所を置き、本殿裏側は杜になっており、陰陽石が祀られる[https://misaka-jinja.com/gallery/ 御坂神社公式HP-Photo]。 === 祭礼日 [https://misaka-jinja.com/event/ 御坂神社公式HP-年間行事予定]=== *{{jalink|1月1日|Q2150}} {{jalink|歳旦祭|Q11546824}} *{{jalink|2月11日|Q2335}} 御弓祭 以前は、{{jalink|旧正月|Q1944136}}初亥の日に行われていたものの、{{jalink|昭和55年}}より{{jalink|建国記念日|Q12981823}}である2月11日に変更された。6名の射手により1回に2本ずつ5回で計10本の{{jalink|矢|Q45922}}が約20m 離れた{{jalink|的|Q11269217}}をめがけて放たれ、的の中心に当たるとその年は{{jalink|五穀豊穣}}となり、的の裏側に封じ込めた「鬼」というところに当たった場合は「[[Wikt:破邪|破邪]]顕正」（はじゃけんしょう）といわれ、邪気をくじき、正しい道理を世間に現し流布するとされる。本来は射手は神社脇の{{jalink|志染川|Q11491046}}にて{{jalink|禊|Q1936265}}を行った後、拝殿で無言で{{jalink|神酒|Q3313722}}を受ける「長床の儀」を行っていたが現在は省略されている。その他、{{jalink|巫女|Q1047139}}による「浦安の舞い」、{{jalink|猿田彦}}による「天地祓い、四方祓い」が奉納され、最後に{{jalink|餅まき|Q11666809}}が行われる。 *{{jalink|2月11日|Q2335}} 祈年祭 *5月4,5日 例祭 2台の{{jalink|屋台|Q2039612}}が奉納される。 *5月例祭後の日曜日 {{jalink|御田祭}} *{{jalink|6月30日|Q2657}} {{jalink|大祓|Q11261910|大祓祭}} *{{jalink|11月23日|Q3021}} {{jalink|新嘗祭|Q11501518}} *{{jalink|12月28日|Q2832}} 大祓祭 === ギャラリー === File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-1.jpg|{{jalink|初詣|Q1032381}}風景 2015年度 File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-6.jpg|{{jalink|拝殿|Q3125745}}から{{jalink|能舞台}}方向を見る Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|WD=Q3029647}} == architectural style (P149) == * {{ill|Q210684|lt=Q210684|WD=Q210684}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|lt=Q17|WD=Q17}} == name in kana (P1814) == * みさかじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6140005006072 == Commons category (P373) == * Misaka-jinja (Miki) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県三木市志染町御坂243', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Misaka-jinja (Miki)}} [[JA:御坂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11488875}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|兵庫県三木市の神社|周防国の式内社|三坂神社 (山口市)}} {{神社 |名称 = 御坂神社 |画像 = [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-3.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|三木市|Q31238}}志染町御坂243 |位置 = {{Coord|34|47|29.1|N|135|03|23.9|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 八戸桂須御諸神 |社格 = {{jalink|式内小社|Q134917287}}・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = 三間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}銅板葺 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 5月3日 |神事 = 御弓祭（2月11日）・御田祭（おんださい）（5月5日） |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御坂神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''御坂神社'''（みさかじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|三木市|Q31238}}志染町御坂243に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に「{{jalink|播磨国|Q1047523}}{{jalink|美嚢郡|Q6868901|美嚢（みのう）郡}}一座 御坂神社」と記載された{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。志染の{{jalink|氏神|Q767655}}。 === 祭神 === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-5.jpg|thumb|right|260px|拝殿から本殿を見る]] '''八戸桂掛須御諸神'''（やとかけすみもろのかみ）を主祭神に{{jalink|大物主神}}と{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|葦原志男神}}を配祀するが、3神とも{{jalink|大国主神}}の別名である[https://misaka-jinja.com/gosaijin/ 御坂神社公式HP-御坂社（御坂神社）の御祭神]。 === 歴史 === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-Honden.jpg|thumb|right|260px|拝殿と本殿]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Nou Butai.jpg|thumb|right|260px|{{jalink|能舞台}}]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-1.jpg|thumb|right|260px|御坂神社の杜。陰陽石が祀られる。]] 鎮座年代は不明。『{{jalink|播磨国風土記|Q11498726}}』には「志染の里三坂にます神八戸掛須御諸神は、大物主と葦原志男の国堅めたまひし以後に天より三坂峯に下り給ひき」との記載があり、第17代天皇、{{jalink|履中天皇|Q329704}}が参拝になったとも伝えられる。その際に、履中天皇が、「この川の流れは大変美しい」といったことにちなみ、この地域一体を「美嚢郡」と呼ぶようになったという。また、天皇の食膳に{{jalink|シジミ|Q1796597}}が這いあがったことから、この地をしじみ（志染）というようになったという[https://misaka-jinja.com/yuisyo/ 御坂神社公式HP-御坂社（御坂神社）の由緒] 。 {{jalink|天正|Q1145058|天正年間}}、{{jalink|豊臣秀吉|Q187550|羽柴秀吉}}が中国討伐の際、その途上に三木の{{jalink|別所長治|Q837435}}の居城{{jalink|三木城|Q11242473|釜山}}攻めを行ったが、そのときの兵火（{{jalink|天正の兵乱}}）により古い記録がすべて焼失したため、詳細が不明になった。その後、{{jalink|慶長|Q1328185}}13年（{{jalink|1608年|Q6758}}）現在地へ社地を移したと伝えられる。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に郷社に列し、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}3年（{{jalink|1914年|Q2083}}）4月{{jalink|神饌幣帛料供進神社}}の指定を受けた。 なお、三木市内には「'''みさか'''」と呼ばれる神社が当御坂神社のほか、'''三坂神社'''、'''美坂神社'''、'''御酒神社'''など計8ヵ所ある。 === 境内 === 本殿ならびに本殿に構造的につながった拝殿とそれらの正面に能舞台と社務所を置き、本殿裏側は杜になっており、陰陽石が祀られる[https://misaka-jinja.com/gallery/ 御坂神社公式HP-Photo]。 === 祭礼日 [https://misaka-jinja.com/event/ 御坂神社公式HP-年間行事予定]=== *{{jalink|1月1日|Q2150}} {{jalink|歳旦祭|Q11546824}} *{{jalink|2月11日|Q2335}} 御弓祭 以前は、{{jalink|旧正月|Q1944136}}初亥の日に行われていたものの、{{jalink|昭和55年}}より{{jalink|建国記念日|Q12981823}}である2月11日に変更された。6名の射手により1回に2本ずつ5回で計10本の{{jalink|矢|Q45922}}が約20m 離れた{{jalink|的|Q11269217}}をめがけて放たれ、的の中心に当たるとその年は{{jalink|五穀豊穣}}となり、的の裏側に封じ込めた「鬼」というところに当たった場合は「[[Wikt:破邪|破邪]]顕正」（はじゃけんしょう）といわれ、邪気をくじき、正しい道理を世間に現し流布するとされる。本来は射手は神社脇の{{jalink|志染川|Q11491046}}にて{{jalink|禊|Q1936265}}を行った後、拝殿で無言で{{jalink|神酒|Q3313722}}を受ける「長床の儀」を行っていたが現在は省略されている。その他、{{jalink|巫女|Q1047139}}による「浦安の舞い」、{{jalink|猿田彦}}による「天地祓い、四方祓い」が奉納され、最後に{{jalink|餅まき|Q11666809}}が行われる。 *{{jalink|2月11日|Q2335}} 祈年祭 *5月4,5日 例祭 2台の{{jalink|屋台|Q2039612}}が奉納される。 *5月例祭後の日曜日 {{jalink|御田祭}} *{{jalink|6月30日|Q2657}} {{jalink|大祓|Q11261910|大祓祭}} *{{jalink|11月23日|Q3021}} {{jalink|新嘗祭|Q11501518}} *{{jalink|12月28日|Q2832}} 大祓祭 === ギャラリー === File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-1.jpg|{{jalink|初詣|Q1032381}}風景 2015年度 File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-6.jpg|{{jalink|拝殿|Q3125745}}から{{jalink|能舞台}}方向を見る File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-2.jpg|{{jalink|陰陽石}}({{jalink|男性|Q8441}}） File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-3.jpg|陰陽石({{jalink|女性|Q467}}） === 参考文献 === *『式内社調査報告』第22巻山陽道、皇學館大學出版部、昭和55年 === 交通 === *自動車：{{jalink|山陽自動車道|Q7413195}}{{jalink|三木東インターチェンジ|Q10866141}}下車、約5分。 === 周辺 === *{{jalink|御坂サイフォン橋|Q11488870}} *{{jalink|志染の石室|Q11491045}} === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === {{Commons|Misaka-jinja (Miki)}} *[https://misaka-jinja.com/ 御坂神社]（神社公式） *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6310024.html 御阪社]（兵庫県神社庁） *[http://www.kita-harima.jp/modules/xdirectory/singlelink.php?cid=110&lid=220 御坂神社]（三木市観光協会） *[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/video/bura1708.htm 風土記の里 志染]（三木市インターネットビデオ放送局「ぶらりと三木へ」） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みさかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:三木市の建築物]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] It is located in [[Harima Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県三木市志染町御坂243', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Misaka-jinja (Miki)}} [[JA:御坂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11488875}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|兵庫県三木市の神社|周防国の式内社|三坂神社 (山口市)}} {{神社 |名称 = 御坂神社 |画像 = [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-3.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|三木市|Q31238}}志染町御坂243 |位置 = {{Coord|34|47|29.1|N|135|03|23.9|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 八戸桂須御諸神 |社格 = {{jalink|式内小社|Q134917287}}・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = 三間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}銅板葺 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 5月3日 |神事 = 御弓祭（2月11日）・御田祭（おんださい）（5月5日） |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御坂神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''御坂神社'''（みさかじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|三木市|Q31238}}志染町御坂243に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に「{{jalink|播磨国|Q1047523}}{{jalink|美嚢郡|Q6868901|美嚢（みのう）郡}}一座 御坂神社」と記載された{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。志染の{{jalink|氏神|Q767655}}。 === 祭神 === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-5.jpg|thumb|right|260px|拝殿から本殿を見る]] '''八戸桂掛須御諸神'''（やとかけすみもろのかみ）を主祭神に{{jalink|大物主神}}と{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|葦原志男神}}を配祀するが、3神とも{{jalink|大国主神}}の別名である[https://misaka-jinja.com/gosaijin/ 御坂神社公式HP-御坂社（御坂神社）の御祭神]。 === 歴史 === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-Honden.jpg|thumb|right|260px|拝殿と本殿]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Nou Butai.jpg|thumb|right|260px|{{jalink|能舞台}}]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-1.jpg|thumb|right|260px|御坂神社の杜。陰陽石が祀られる。]] 鎮座年代は不明。『{{jalink|播磨国風土記|Q11498726}}』には「志染の里三坂にます神八戸掛須御諸神は、大物主と葦原志男の国堅めたまひし以後に天より三坂峯に下り給ひき」との記載があり、第17代天皇、{{jalink|履中天皇|Q329704}}が参拝になったとも伝えられる。その際に、履中天皇が、「この川の流れは大変美しい」といったことにちなみ、この地域一体を「美嚢郡」と呼ぶようになったという。また、天皇の食膳に{{jalink|シジミ|Q1796597}}が這いあがったことから、この地をしじみ（志染）というようになったという[https://misaka-jinja.com/yuisyo/ 御坂神社公式HP-御坂社（御坂神社）の由緒] 。 {{jalink|天正|Q1145058|天正年間}}、{{jalink|豊臣秀吉|Q187550|羽柴秀吉}}が中国討伐の際、その途上に三木の{{jalink|別所長治|Q837435}}の居城{{jalink|三木城|Q11242473|釜山}}攻めを行ったが、そのときの兵火（{{jalink|天正の兵乱}}）により古い記録がすべて焼失したため、詳細が不明になった。その後、{{jalink|慶長|Q1328185}}13年（{{jalink|1608年|Q6758}}）現在地へ社地を移したと伝えられる。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に郷社に列し、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}3年（{{jalink|1914年|Q2083}}）4月{{jalink|神饌幣帛料供進神社}}の指定を受けた。 なお、三木市内には「'''みさか'''」と呼ばれる神社が当御坂神社のほか、'''三坂神社'''、'''美坂神社'''、'''御酒神社'''など計8ヵ所ある。 === 境内 === 本殿ならびに本殿に構造的につながった拝殿とそれらの正面に能舞台と社務所を置き、本殿裏側は杜になっており、陰陽石が祀られる[https://misaka-jinja.com/gallery/ 御坂神社公式HP-Photo]。 === 祭礼日 [https://misaka-jinja.com/event/ 御坂神社公式HP-年間行事予定]=== *{{jalink|1月1日|Q2150}} {{jalink|歳旦祭|Q11546824}} *{{jalink|2月11日|Q2335}} 御弓祭 以前は、{{jalink|旧正月|Q1944136}}初亥の日に行われていたものの、{{jalink|昭和55年}}より{{jalink|建国記念日|Q12981823}}である2月11日に変更された。6名の射手により1回に2本ずつ5回で計10本の{{jalink|矢|Q45922}}が約20m 離れた{{jalink|的|Q11269217}}をめがけて放たれ、的の中心に当たるとその年は{{jalink|五穀豊穣}}となり、的の裏側に封じ込めた「鬼」というところに当たった場合は「[[Wikt:破邪|破邪]]顕正」（はじゃけんしょう）といわれ、邪気をくじき、正しい道理を世間に現し流布するとされる。本来は射手は神社脇の{{jalink|志染川|Q11491046}}にて{{jalink|禊|Q1936265}}を行った後、拝殿で無言で{{jalink|神酒|Q3313722}}を受ける「長床の儀」を行っていたが現在は省略されている。その他、{{jalink|巫女|Q1047139}}による「浦安の舞い」、{{jalink|猿田彦}}による「天地祓い、四方祓い」が奉納され、最後に{{jalink|餅まき|Q11666809}}が行われる。 *{{jalink|2月11日|Q2335}} 祈年祭 *5月4,5日 例祭 2台の{{jalink|屋台|Q2039612}}が奉納される。 *5月例祭後の日曜日 {{jalink|御田祭}} *{{jalink|6月30日|Q2657}} {{jalink|大祓|Q11261910|大祓祭}} *{{jalink|11月23日|Q3021}} {{jalink|新嘗祭|Q11501518}} *{{jalink|12月28日|Q2832}} 大祓祭 === ギャラリー === File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-1.jpg|{{jalink|初詣|Q1032381}}風景 2015年度 File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-6.jpg|{{jalink|拝殿|Q3125745}}から{{jalink|能舞台}}方向を見る File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-2.jpg|{{jalink|陰陽石}}({{jalink|男性|Q8441}}） File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-3.jpg|陰陽石({{jalink|女性|Q467}}） === 参考文献 === *『式内社調査報告』第22巻山陽道、皇學館大學出版部、昭和55年 === 交通 === *自動車：{{jalink|山陽自動車道|Q7413195}}{{jalink|三木東インターチェンジ|Q10866141}}下車、約5分。 === 周辺 === *{{jalink|御坂サイフォン橋|Q11488870}} *{{jalink|志染の石室|Q11491045}} === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === {{Commons|Misaka-jinja (Miki)}} *[https://misaka-jinja.com/ 御坂神社]（神社公式） *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6310024.html 御阪社]（兵庫県神社庁） *[http://www.kita-harima.jp/modules/xdirectory/singlelink.php?cid=110&lid=220 御坂神社]（三木市観光協会） *[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/video/bura1708.htm 風土記の里 志染]（三木市インターネットビデオ放送局「ぶらりと三木へ」） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みさかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated 御坂神社」と記載された{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。志染の{{jalink|氏神|Q767655}}。 === 祭神 === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-5.jpg|thumb|right|260px|拝殿から本殿を見る]] '''八戸桂掛須御諸神'''（やとかけすみもろのかみ）を主祭神に{{jalink|大物主神}}と{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|葦原志男神}}を配祀するが、3神とも{{jalink|大国主神}}の別名である[https://misaka-jinja.com/gosaijin/ 御坂神社公式HP-御坂社（御坂神社）の御祭神]。 === 歴史 === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-Honden.jpg|thumb|right|260px|拝殿と本殿]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Nou Butai.jpg|thumb|right|260px|{{jalink|能舞台}}]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-1.jpg|thumb|right|260px|御坂神社の杜。陰陽石が祀られる。]] 鎮座年代は不明。『{{jalink|播磨国風土記|Q11498726}}』には「志染の里三坂にます神八戸掛須御諸神は、大物主と葦原志男の国堅めたまひし以後に天より三坂峯に下り給ひき」との記載があり、第17代天皇、{{jalink|履中天皇|Q329704}}が参拝になったとも伝えられる。その際に、履中天皇が、「この川の流れは大変美しい」といったことにちなみ、この地域一体を「美嚢郡」と呼ぶようになったという。また、天皇の食膳に{{jalink|シジミ|Q1796597}}が這いあがったことから、この地をしじみ（志染）というようになったという[https://misaka-jinja.com/yuisyo/ 御坂神社公式HP-御坂社（御坂神社）の由緒] 。 {{jalink|天正|Q1145058|天正年間}}、{{jalink|豊臣秀吉|Q187550|羽柴秀吉}}が中国討伐の際、その途上に三木の{{jalink|別所長治|Q837435}}の居城{{jalink|三木城|Q11242473|釜山}}攻めを行ったが、そのときの兵火（{{jalink|天正の兵乱}}）により古い記録がすべて焼失したため、詳細が不明になった。その後、{{jalink|慶長|Q1328185}}13年（{{jalink|1608年|Q6758}}）現在地へ社地を移したと伝えられる。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に郷社に列し、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}3年（{{jalink|1914年|Q2083}}）4月{{jalink|神饌幣帛料供進神社}}の指定を受けた。 なお、三木市内には「'''みさか'''」と呼ばれる神社が当御坂神社のほか、'''三坂神社'''、'''美坂神社'''、'''御酒神社'''など計8ヵ所ある。 === 境内 === 本殿ならびに本殿に構造的につながった拝殿とそれらの正面に能舞台と社務所を置き、本殿裏側は杜になっており、陰陽石が祀られる[https://misaka-jinja.com/gallery/ 御坂神社公式HP-Photo]。 === 祭礼日 [https://misaka-jinja.com/event/ 御坂神社公式HP-年間行事予定]=== *{{jalink|1月1日|Q2150}} {{jalink|歳旦祭|Q11546824}} *{{jalink|2月11日|Q2335}} 御弓祭 以前は、{{jalink|旧正月|Q1944136}}初亥の日に行われていたものの、{{jalink|昭和55年}}より{{jalink|建国記念日|Q12981823}}である2月11日に変更された。6名の射手により1回に2本ずつ5回で計10本の{{jalink|矢|Q45922}}が約20m 離れた{{jalink|的|Q11269217}}をめがけて放たれ、的の中心に当たるとその年は{{jalink|五穀豊穣}}となり、的の裏側に封じ込めた「鬼」というところに当たった場合は「[[Wikt:破邪|破邪]]顕正」（はじゃけんしょう）といわれ、邪気をくじき、正しい道理を世間に現し流布するとされる。本来は射手は神社脇の{{jalink|志染川|Q11491046}}にて{{jalink|禊|Q1936265}}を行った後、拝殿で無言で{{jalink|神酒|Q3313722}}を受ける「長床の儀」を行っていたが現在は省略されている。その他、{{jalink|巫女|Q1047139}}による「浦安の舞い」、{{jalink|猿田彦}}による「天地祓い、四方祓い」が奉納され、最後に{{jalink|餅まき|Q11666809}}が行われる。 *{{jalink|2月11日|Q2335}} 祈年祭 *5月4,5日 例祭 2台の{{jalink|屋台|Q2039612}}が奉納される。 *5月例祭後の日曜日 {{jalink|御田祭}} *{{jalink|6月30日|Q2657}} {{jalink|大祓|Q11261910|大祓祭}} *{{jalink|11月23日|Q3021}} {{jalink|新嘗祭|Q11501518}} *{{jalink|12月28日|Q2832}} 大祓祭 === ギャラリー === File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-1.jpg|{{jalink|初詣|Q1032381}}風景 2015年度 File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-6.jpg|{{jalink|拝殿|Q3125745}}から{{jalink|能舞台}}方向を見る File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-2.jpg|{{jalink|陰陽石}}({{jalink|男性|Q8441}}） File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-3.jpg|陰陽石({{jalink|女性|Q467}}） === 参考文献 === *『式内社調査報告』第22巻山陽道、皇學館大學出版部、昭和55年 === 交通 === *自動車：{{jalink|山陽自動車道|Q7413195}}{{jalink|三木東インターチェンジ|Q10866141}}下車、約5分。 === 周辺 === *{{jalink|御坂サイフォン橋|Q11488870}} *{{jalink|志染の石室|Q11491045}} === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === {{Commons|Misaka-jinja (Miki)}} *[https://misaka-jinja.com/ 御坂神社]（神社公式） *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6310024.html 御阪社]（兵庫県神社庁） *[http://www.kita-harima.jp/modules/xdirectory/singlelink.php?cid=110&lid=220 御坂神社]（三木市観光協会） *[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/video/bura1708.htm 風土記の里 志染]（三木市インターネットビデオ放送局「ぶらりと三木へ」） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みさかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Misaka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Misaka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] It is located in [[Harima Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|lt=Q17|WD=Q17}} == name in kana (P1814) == * みさかじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6140005006072 == Commons category (P373) == * Misaka-jinja (Miki) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県三木市志染町御坂243', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Misaka-jinja (Miki)}} [[JA:御坂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11488875}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|兵庫県三木市の神社|周防国の式内社|三坂神社 (山口市)}} {{神社 |名称 = 御坂神社 |画像 = [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-3.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地 = {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}{{jalink|Miki|Q31238}}, Shijimi-chō Misaka 243 |位置 = {{Coord|34|47|29.1|N|135|03|23.9|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinai Shōsha|Q134917287}}; former {{jalink|district shrine|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Three-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}}, copper-plate roofing |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = May 3 |神事 = Oyumi-sai (February 11); {{jalink|Onda-sai}} (May 5) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御坂神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Misaka Shrine''' (みさかじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} located at Shijimi-chō Misaka 243, {{jalink|Miki|Q31238}}, {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}. It is listed in the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'' as "{{jalink|Harima Province|Q1047523}}, {{jalink|Minō District|Q6868901|Minō (Minō) District}}, one seat, Misaka Shrine," making it a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine ranking}} was {{jalink|district shrine|Q134917316}}. It is the {{jalink|ujigami|Q767655}} of Shijimi. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-5.jpg|thumb|right|260px|View of the main hall from the worship hall]] The principal enshrined deity is '''Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami''' (やとかけすみもろのかみ), with {{jalink|Ōmononushi}} and {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ashiharashio no Kami}} enshrined alongside. All three are alternative names for {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944}}[https://misaka-jinja.com/gosaijin/ Misaka Shrine official website - Enshrined deities of Misaka Shrine]. === History === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-Honden.jpg|thumb|right|260px|Worship hall and main hall]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Nou Butai.jpg|thumb|right|260px|{{jalink|Noh stage}}]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-1.jpg|thumb|right|260px|The grove of Misaka Shrine, where the yin-yang stones are enshrined.]] The date of establishment is unknown. The ''{{jalink|Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726}}'' records: "The deity Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami, residing at Misaka in the village of Shijimi, descended from heaven to the peak of Misaka after Ōmononushi and Ashiharashio had finished pacifying the land." It is also said that the 17th Emperor, {{jalink|Emperor Richū|Q329704}}, visited the shrine. On that occasion, Emperor Richū reportedly said, "The flow of this river is exceedingly beautiful," and from this the entire region came to be called "Minō District." Furthermore, because a {{jalink|freshwater clam|Q1796597}} crawled up onto the Emperor's dining table, the area came to be known as Shijimi (志染)[https://misaka-jinja.com/yuisyo/ Misaka Shrine official website - History of Misaka Shrine] . During the {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058|Tenshō era}}, when {{jalink|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|Hashiba Hideyoshi}} was conducting his campaign against the western provinces, he laid siege to {{jalink|Miki Castle|Q11242473|Kamayama}}, the stronghold of {{jalink|Bessho Nagaharu|Q837435}} in Miki. The fires of war from that time (the {{jalink|Tenshō Disturbance}}) destroyed all the ancient records, making the details unclear. It is said that the shrine was subsequently relocated to its present site in {{jalink|Keichō|Q1328185}} 13 ({{jalink|1608|Q6758}}). In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), the shrine was ranked as a district shrine, and in {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 3 ({{jalink|1914|Q2083}}) April, it was designated as a {{jalink|shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and cloth}}. Within Miki City, there are a total of eight shrines called "'''Misaka'''," including this Misaka Shrine, as well as '''Misaka Shrine''' (三坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (美坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (御酒神社), and others. === Precincts === The main hall and the worship hall, which is structurally connected to it, face a {{jalink|Noh stage}} and a shrine office in front. The area behind the main hall is a grove where {{jalink|yin-yang stones}} are enshrined[https://misaka-jinja.com/gallery/ Misaka Shrine official website - Photo]. === Festival calendar [https://misaka-jinja.com/event/ Misaka Shrine official website - Annual events schedule]=== *{{jalink|January 1|Q2150}} {{jalink|Saitan-sai|Q11546824}} *{{jalink|February 11|Q2335}} Oyumi-sai (Archery Festival). Previously held on the first Day of the Boar after {{jalink|Lunar New Year|Q1944136}}, it was changed to February 11, {{jalink|National Foundation Day|Q12981823}}, from {{jalink|Shōwa 55}}. Six archers each shoot 2 {{jalink|arrow|Q45922}}s per round over 5 rounds, for a total of 10 arrows aimed at a {{jalink|target|Q11269217}} approximately 20 meters away. If an arrow hits the center of the target, it foretells a {{jalink|bountiful harvest}} for that year. If it hits the area marked "oni" (demon) sealed on the back of the target, it is called "[[Wikt:破邪|haja]] kenshō" (breaking evil and revealing righteousness), meaning that evil is defeated and correct principles are made manifest in the world. Originally, the archers would perform {{jalink|misogi|Q1936265}} in the {{jalink|Shijimi River|Q11491046}} beside the shrine, then silently receive {{jalink|sacred sake|Q3313722}} in the "Nagatoko no Gi" ceremony at the {{jalink|worship hall|Q3125745}}, but this is now omitted. In addition, the "Urayasu no Mai" dance is performed by a {{jalink|miko|Q1047139}}, {{jalink|Sarutahiko}} performs the "Tenchi-barai, Shihō-barai" (purification of heaven and earth, purification of the four directions), and finally {{jalink|mochi-maki|Q11666809}} (mochi-throwing) is held. *{{jalink|February 11|Q2335}} Kinen-sai (Prayer Festival) *May 4-5, Annual Festival. Two {{jalink|festival float|Q2039612}}s are dedicated. *Sunday following the May annual festival: {{jalink|Onda-sai}} *{{jalink|June 30|Q2657}} {{jalink|Ōharae|Q11261910|Ōharae-sai}} *{{jalink|November 23|Q3021}} {{jalink|Niiname-sai|Q11501518}} *{{jalink|December 28|Q2832}} Ōharae-sai === Gallery === File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-1.jpg|{{jalink|hatsumōde|Q1032381}} scene, 2015 File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-6.jpg|View from the {{jalink|worship hall|Q3125745}} toward the {{jalink|Noh stage}} File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-2.jpg|{{jalink|yin-yang stones}} ({{jalink|male|Q8441}}) File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-3.jpg|Yin-yang stones ({{jalink|female|Q467}}) === References === *''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' (Report on Shikinaisha Surveys), Vol. 22, San'yōdō, Kōgakkan University Press, Shōwa 55 === Access === *By car: Approximately 5 minutes from {{jalink|Miki-Higashi IC|Q10866141}} on the {{jalink|San'yō Expressway|Q7413195}}. === Surroundings === *{{jalink|Misaka Siphon Bridge|Q11488870}} *{{jalink|Shijimi Stone Chamber|Q11491045}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commons|Misaka-jinja (Miki)}} *[https://misaka-jinja.com/ Misaka Shrine] (official shrine website) *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6310024.html Misaka Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Agency) *[http://www.kita-harima.jp/modules/xdirectory/singlelink.php?cid=110&lid=220 Misaka Shrine] (Miki City Tourism Association) *[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/video/bura1708.htm Shijimi, the land of Fudoki] (Miki City Internet Video Station "Burari to Miki e") {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みさかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Misaka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Misaka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:三木市の建築物]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県三木市志染町御坂243', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Misaka-jinja (Miki)}} [[JA:御坂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11488875}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|兵庫県三木市の神社|周防国の式内社|三坂神社 (山口市)}} {{神社 |名称 = 御坂神社 |画像 = [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-3.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地 = {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}{{jalink|Miki|Q31238}}, Shijimi-chō Misaka 243 |位置 = {{Coord|34|47|29.1|N|135|03|23.9|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinai Shōsha|Q134917287}}; former {{jalink|district shrine|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Three-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}}, copper-plate roofing |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = May 3 |神事 = Oyumi-sai (February 11); {{jalink|Onda-sai}} (May 5) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御坂神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Misaka Shrine''' (みさかじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} located at Shijimi-chō Misaka 243, {{jalink|Miki|Q31238}}, {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}. It is listed in the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'' as "{{jalink|Harima Province|Q1047523}}, {{jalink|Minō District|Q6868901|Minō (Minō) District}}, one seat, Misaka Shrine," making it a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine ranking}} was {{jalink|district shrine|Q134917316}}. It is the {{jalink|ujigami|Q767655}} of Shijimi. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-5.jpg|thumb|right|260px|View of the main hall from the worship hall]] The principal enshrined deity is '''Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami''' (やとかけすみもろのかみ), with {{jalink|Ōmononushi}} and {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ashiharashio no Kami}} enshrined alongside. All three are alternative names for {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944}}[https://misaka-jinja.com/gosaijin/ Misaka Shrine official website - Enshrined deities of Misaka Shrine]. === History === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-Honden.jpg|thumb|right|260px|Worship hall and main hall]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Nou Butai.jpg|thumb|right|260px|{{jalink|Noh stage}}]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-1.jpg|thumb|right|260px|The grove of Misaka Shrine, where the yin-yang stones are enshrined.]] The date of establishment is unknown. The ''{{jalink|Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726}}'' records: "The deity Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami, residing at Misaka in the village of Shijimi, descended from heaven to the peak of Misaka after Ōmononushi and Ashiharashio had finished pacifying the land." It is also said that the 17th Emperor, {{jalink|Emperor Richū|Q329704}}, visited the shrine. On that occasion, Emperor Richū reportedly said, "The flow of this river is exceedingly beautiful," and from this the entire region came to be called "Minō District." Furthermore, because a {{jalink|freshwater clam|Q1796597}} crawled up onto the Emperor's dining table, the area came to be known as Shijimi (志染)[https://misaka-jinja.com/yuisyo/ Misaka Shrine official website - History of Misaka Shrine] . During the {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058|Tenshō era}}, when {{jalink|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|Hashiba Hideyoshi}} was conducting his campaign against the western provinces, he laid siege to {{jalink|Miki Castle|Q11242473|Kamayama}}, the stronghold of {{jalink|Bessho Nagaharu|Q837435}} in Miki. The fires of war from that time (the {{jalink|Tenshō Disturbance}}) destroyed all the ancient records, making the details unclear. It is said that the shrine was subsequently relocated to its present site in {{jalink|Keichō|Q1328185}} 13 ({{jalink|1608|Q6758}}). In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), the shrine was ranked as a district shrine, and in {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 3 ({{jalink|1914|Q2083}}) April, it was designated as a {{jalink|shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and cloth}}. Within Miki City, there are a total of eight shrines called "'''Misaka'''," including this Misaka Shrine, as well as '''Misaka Shrine''' (三坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (美坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (御酒神社), and others. === Precincts === The main hall and the worship hall, which is structurally connected to it, face a {{jalink|Noh stage}} and a shrine office in front. The area behind the main hall is a grove where {{jalink|yin-yang stones}} are enshrined[https://misaka-jinja.com/gallery/ Misaka Shrine official website - Photo]. === Festival calendar [https://misaka-jinja.com/event/ Misaka Shrine official website - Annual events schedule]=== *{{jalink|January 1|Q2150}} {{jalink|Saitan-sai|Q11546824}} *{{jalink|February 11|Q2335}} Oyumi-sai (Archery Festival). Previously held on the first Day of the Boar after {{jalink|Lunar New Year|Q1944136}}, it was changed to February 11, {{jalink|National Foundation Day|Q12981823}}, from {{jalink|Shōwa 55}}. Six archers each shoot 2 {{jalink|arrow|Q45922}}s per round over 5 rounds, for a total of 10 arrows aimed at a {{jalink|target|Q11269217}} approximately 20 meters away. If an arrow hits the center of the target, it foretells a {{jalink|bountiful harvest}} for that year. If it hits the area marked "oni" (demon) sealed on the back of the target, it is called "[[Wikt:破邪|haja]] kenshō" (breaking evil and revealing righteousness), meaning that evil is defeated and correct principles are made manifest in the world. Originally, the archers would perform {{jalink|misogi|Q1936265}} in the {{jalink|Shijimi River|Q11491046}} beside the shrine, then silently receive {{jalink|sacred sake|Q3313722}} in the "Nagatoko no Gi" ceremony at the {{jalink|worship hall|Q3125745}}, but this is now omitted. In addition, the "Urayasu no Mai" dance is performed by a {{jalink|miko|Q1047139}}, {{jalink|Sarutahiko}} performs the "Tenchi-barai, Shihō-barai" (purification of heaven and earth, purification of the four directions), and finally {{jalink|mochi-maki|Q11666809}} (mochi-throwing) is held. *{{jalink|February 11|Q2335}} Kinen-sai (Prayer Festival) *May 4-5, Annual Festival. Two {{jalink|festival float|Q2039612}}s are dedicated. *Sunday following the May annual festival: {{jalink|Onda-sai}} *{{jalink|June 30|Q2657}} {{jalink|Ōharae|Q11261910|Ōharae-sai}} *{{jalink|November 23|Q3021}} {{jalink|Niiname-sai|Q11501518}} *{{jalink|December 28|Q2832}} Ōharae-sai === Gallery === File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-1.jpg|{{jalink|hatsumōde|Q1032381}} scene, 2015 File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-6.jpg|View from the {{jalink|worship hall|Q3125745}} toward the {{jalink|Noh stage}} File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-2.jpg|{{jalink|yin-yang stones}} ({{jalink|male|Q8441}}) File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-3.jpg|Yin-yang stones ({{jalink|female|Q467}}) === References === *''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' (Report on Shikinaisha Surveys), Vol. 22, San'yōdō, Kōgakkan University Press, Shōwa 55 === Access === *By car: Approximately 5 minutes from {{jalink|Miki-Higashi IC|Q10866141}} on the {{jalink|San'yō Expressway|Q7413195}}. === Surroundings === *{{jalink|Misaka Siphon Bridge|Q11488870}} *{{jalink|Shijimi Stone Chamber|Q11491045}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commons|Misaka-jinja (Miki)}} *[https://misaka-jinja.com/ Misaka Shrine] (official shrine website) *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6310024.html Misaka Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Agency) *[http://www.kita-harima.jp/modules/xdirectory/singlelink.php?cid=110&lid=220 Misaka Shrine] (Miki City Tourism Association) *[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/video/bura1708.htm Shijimi, the land of Fudoki] (Miki City Internet Video Station "Burari to Miki e") {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みさかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Misaka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Misaka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:三木市の建築物]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

Two {{jalink|festival float|Q2039612}}s are dedicated. *Sunday following the May annual festival: {{jalink|Onda-sai}} *{{jalink|June 30|Q2657}} {{jalink|Ōharae|Q11261910|Ōharae-sai}} *{{jalink|November 23|Q3021}} {{jalink|Niiname-sai|Q11501518}} *{{jalink|December 28|Q2832}} Ōharae-sai === Gallery === File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-1.jpg|{{jalink|hatsumōde|Q1032381}} scene, 2015 File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-6.jpg|View from the {{jalink|worship hall|Q3125745}} toward the {{jalink|Noh stage}} File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-2.jpg|{{jalink|yin-yang stones}} ({{jalink|male|Q8441}}) File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-3.jpg|Yin-yang stones ({{jalink|female|Q467}}) === References === *''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' (Report on Shikinaisha Surveys), Vol. 22, San'yōdō, Kōgakkan University Press, Shōwa 55 === Access === *By car: Approximately 5 minutes from {{jalink|Miki-Higashi IC|Q10866141}} on the {{jalink|San'yō Expressway|Q7413195}}. === Surroundings === *{{jalink|Misaka Siphon Bridge|Q11488870}} *{{jalink|Shijimi Stone Chamber|Q11491045}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commons|Misaka-jinja (Miki)}} *[https://misaka-jinja.com/ Misaka Shrine] (official shrine website) *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6310024.html Misaka Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Agency) *[http://www.kita-harima.jp/modules/xdirectory/singlelink.php?cid=110&lid=220 Misaka Shrine] (Miki City Tourism Association) *[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/video/bura1708.htm Shijimi, the land of Fudoki] (Miki City Internet Video Station "Burari to Miki e") {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みさかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Misaka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Misaka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:三木市の建築物]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: used local AI agents to translate these pages. Contrary to the description it was actually Claude Code for a few, OpenClaw with Codex, and regular Codex. Ollama and DeepSeek were too bad to actually use. see categorization since some of them are very questionable, especially if there was a massive decrease in page size or the {{jawiki| template is minimal or absent on the page

It is located in [[Harima Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|WD=Q3029647}} == architectural style (P149) == * {{ill|Q210684|lt=Q210684|WD=Q210684}} == address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県三木市志染町御坂243', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Misaka-jinja (Miki)}} [[JA:御坂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11488875}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|兵庫県三木市の神社|周防国の式内社|三坂神社 (山口市)}} {{神社 |名称 = 御坂神社 |画像 = [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-3.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地 = {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}{{jalink|Miki|Q31238}}, Shijimi-chō Misaka 243 |位置 = {{Coord|34|47|29.1|N|135|03|23.9|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinai Shōsha|Q134917287}}; former {{jalink|district shrine|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Three-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}}, copper-plate roofing |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = May 3 |神事 = Oyumi-sai (February 11); {{jalink|Onda-sai}} (May 5) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御坂神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Misaka Shrine''' (みさかじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} located at Shijimi-chō Misaka 243, {{jalink|Miki|Q31238}}, {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}. It is listed in the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'' as "{{jalink|Harima Province|Q1047523}}, {{jalink|Minō District|Q6868901|Minō (Minō) District}}, one seat, Misaka Shrine," making it a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine ranking}} was {{jalink|district shrine|Q134917316}}. It is the {{jalink|ujigami|Q767655}} of Shijimi. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-5.jpg|thumb|right|260px|View of the main hall from the worship hall]] The principal enshrined deity is '''Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami''' (やとかけすみもろのかみ), with {{jalink|Ōmononushi}} and {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ashiharashio no Kami}} enshrined alongside. All three are alternative names for {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944}}[https://misaka-jinja.com/gosaijin/ Misaka Shrine official website - Enshrined deities of Misaka Shrine]. === History === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-Honden.jpg|thumb|right|260px|Worship hall and main hall]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Nou Butai.jpg|thumb|right|260px|{{jalink|Noh stage}}]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-1.jpg|thumb|right|260px|The grove of Misaka Shrine, where the yin-yang stones are enshrined.]] The date of establishment is unknown. The ''{{jalink|Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726}}'' records: "The deity Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami, residing at Misaka in the village of Shijimi, descended from heaven to the peak of Misaka after Ōmononushi and Ashiharashio had finished pacifying the land." It is also said that the 17th Emperor, {{jalink|Emperor Richū|Q329704}}, visited the shrine. On that occasion, Emperor Richū reportedly said, "The flow of this river is exceedingly beautiful," and from this the entire region came to be called "Minō District." Furthermore, because a {{jalink|freshwater clam|Q1796597}} crawled up onto the Emperor's dining table, the area came to be known as Shijimi (志染)[https://misaka-jinja.com/yuisyo/ Misaka Shrine official website - History of Misaka Shrine] . During the {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058|Tenshō era}}, when {{jalink|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|Hashiba Hideyoshi}} was conducting his campaign against the western provinces, he laid siege to {{jalink|Miki Castle|Q11242473|Kamayama}}, the stronghold of {{jalink|Bessho Nagaharu|Q837435}} in Miki. The fires of war from that time (the {{jalink|Tenshō Disturbance}}) destroyed all the ancient records, making the details unclear. It is said that the shrine was subsequently relocated to its present site in {{jalink|Keichō|Q1328185}} 13 ({{jalink|1608|Q6758}}). In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), the shrine was ranked as a district shrine, and in {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 3 ({{jalink|1914|Q2083}}) April, it was designated as a {{jalink|shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and cloth}}. Within Miki City, there are a total of eight shrines called "'''Misaka'''," including this Misaka Shrine, as well as '''Misaka Shrine''' (三坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (美坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (御酒神社), and others. === Precincts === The main hall and the worship hall, which is structurally connected to it, face a {{jalink|Noh stage}} and a shrine office in front. The area behind the main hall is a grove where {{jalink|yin-yang stones}} are enshrined[https://misaka-jinja.com/gallery/ Misaka Shrine official website - Photo]. === Festival calendar [https://misaka-jinja.com/event/ Misaka Shrine official website - Annual events schedule]=== *{{jalink|January 1|Q2150}} {{jalink|Saitan-sai|Q11546824}} *{{jalink|February 11|Q2335}} Oyumi-sai (Archery Festival). Previously held on the first Day of the Boar after {{jalink|Lunar New Year|Q1944136}}, it was changed to February 11, {{jalink|National Foundation Day|Q12981823}}, from {{jalink|Shōwa 55}}. Six archers each shoot 2 {{jalink|arrow|Q45922}}s per round over 5 rounds, for a total of 10 arrows aimed at a {{jalink|target|Q11269217}} approximately 20 meters away. If an arrow hits the center of the target, it foretells a {{jalink|bountiful harvest}} for that year. If it hits the area marked "oni" (demon) sealed on the back of the target, it is called "[[Wikt:破邪|haja]] kenshō" (breaking evil and revealing righteousness), meaning that evil is defeated and correct principles are made manifest in the world. Originally, the archers would perform {{jalink|misogi|Q1936265}} in the {{jalink|Shijimi River|Q11491046}} beside the shrine, then silently receive {{jalink|sacred sake|Q3313722}} in the "Nagatoko no Gi" ceremony at the {{jalink|worship hall|Q3125745}}, but this is now omitted. In addition, the "Urayasu no Mai" dance is performed by a {{jalink|miko|Q1047139}}, {{jalink|Sarutahiko}} performs the "Tenchi-barai, Shihō-barai" (purification of heaven and earth, purification of the four directions), and finally {{jalink|mochi-maki|Q11666809}} (mochi-throwing) is held. *{{jalink|February 11|Q2335}} Kinen-sai (Prayer Festival) *May 4-5, Annual Festival. Two {{jalink|festival float|Q2039612}}s are dedicated. *Sunday following the May annual festival: {{jalink|Onda-sai}} *{{jalink|June 30|Q2657}} {{jalink|Ōharae|Q11261910|Ōharae-sai}} *{{jalink|November 23|Q3021}} {{jalink|Niiname-sai|Q11501518}} *{{jalink|December 28|Q2832}} Ōharae-sai === Gallery === File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-1.jpg|{{jalink|hatsumōde|Q1032381}} scene, 2015 File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-6.jpg|View from the {{jalink|worship hall|Q3125745}} toward the {{jalink|Noh stage}} File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-2.jpg|{{jalink|yin-yang stones}} ({{jalink|male|Q8441}}) File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-3.jpg|Yin-yang stones ({{jalink|female|Q467}}) === References === *''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' (Report on Shikinaisha Surveys), Vol. 22, San'yōdō, Kōgakkan University Press, Shōwa 55 === Access === *By car: Approximately 5 minutes from {{jalink|Miki-Higashi IC|Q10866141}} on the {{jalink|San'yō Expressway|Q7413195}}. === Surroundings === *{{jalink|Misaka Siphon Bridge|Q11488870}} *{{jalink|Shijimi Stone Chamber|Q11491045}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commons|Misaka-jinja (Miki)}} *[https://misaka-jinja.com/ Misaka Shrine] (official shrine website) *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6310024.html Misaka Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Agency) *[http://www.kita-harima.jp/modules/xdirectory/singlelink.php?cid=110&lid=220 Misaka Shrine] (Miki City Tourism Association) *[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/video/bura1708.htm Shijimi, the land of Fudoki] (Miki City Internet Video Station "Burari to Miki e") {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みさかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Misaka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Misaka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:三木市の建築物]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

On that occasion, Emperor Richū reportedly said, "The flow of this river is exceedingly beautiful," and from this the entire region came to be called "Minō District." Furthermore, because a {{jalink|freshwater clam|Q1796597}} crawled up onto the Emperor's dining table, the area came to be known as Shijimi (志染)[https://misaka-jinja.com/yuisyo/ Misaka Shrine official website - History of Misaka Shrine] . During the {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058|Tenshō era}}, when {{jalink|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|Hashiba Hideyoshi}} was conducting his campaign against the western provinces, he laid siege to {{jalink|Miki Castle|Q11242473|Kamayama}}, the stronghold of {{jalink|Bessho Nagaharu|Q837435}} in Miki. The fires of war from that time (the {{jalink|Tenshō Disturbance}}) destroyed all the ancient records, making the details unclear. It is said that the shrine was subsequently relocated to its present site in {{jalink|Keichō|Q1328185}} 13 ({{jalink|1608|Q6758}}). In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), the shrine was ranked as a district shrine, and in {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 3 ({{jalink|1914|Q2083}}) April, it was designated as a {{jalink|shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and cloth}}. Within Miki City, there are a total of eight shrines called "'''Misaka'''," including this Misaka Shrine, as well as '''Misaka Shrine''' (三坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (美坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (御酒神社), and others. === Precincts === The main hall and the worship hall, which is structurally connected to it, face a {{jalink|Noh stage}} and a shrine office in front. The area behind the main hall is a grove where {{jalink|yin-yang stones}} are enshrined[https://misaka-jinja.com/gallery/ Misaka Shrine official website - Photo]. === Festival calendar [https://misaka-jinja.com/event/ Misaka Shrine official website - Annual events schedule]=== *{{jalink|January 1|Q2150}} {{jalink|Saitan-sai|Q11546824}} *{{jalink|February 11|Q2335}} Oyumi-sai (Archery Festival). Previously held on the first Day of the Boar after {{jalink|Lunar New Year|Q1944136}}, it was changed to February 11, {{jalink|National Foundation Day|Q12981823}}, from {{jalink|Shōwa 55}}. Six archers each shoot 2 {{jalink|arrow|Q45922}}s per round over 5 rounds, for a total of 10 arrows aimed at a {{jalink|target|Q11269217}} approximately 20 meters away. If an arrow hits the center of the target, it foretells a {{jalink|bountiful harvest}} for that year. If it hits the area marked "oni" (demon) sealed on the back of the target, it is called "[[Wikt:破邪|haja]] kenshō" (breaking evil and revealing righteousness), meaning that evil is defeated and correct principles are made manifest in the world. Originally, the archers would perform {{jalink|misogi|Q1936265}} in the {{jalink|Shijimi River|Q11491046}} beside the shrine, then silently receive {{jalink|sacred sake|Q3313722}} in the "Nagatoko no Gi" ceremony at the {{jalink|worship hall|Q3125745}}, but this is now omitted. In addition, the "Urayasu no Mai" dance is performed by a {{jalink|miko|Q1047139}}, {{jalink|Sarutahiko}} performs the "Tenchi-barai, Shihō-barai" (purification of heaven and earth, purification of the four directions), and finally {{jalink|mochi-maki|Q11666809}} (mochi-throwing) is held. *{{jalink|February 11|Q2335}} Kinen-sai (Prayer Festival) *May 4-5, Annual Festival. Two {{jalink|festival float|Q2039612}}s are dedicated. *Sunday following the May annual festival: {{jalink|Onda-sai}} *{{jalink|June 30|Q2657}} {{jalink|Ōharae|Q11261910|Ōharae-sai}} *{{jalink|November 23|Q3021}} {{jalink|Niiname-sai|Q11501518}} *{{jalink|December 28|Q2832}} Ōharae-sai === Gallery === File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-1.jpg|{{jalink|hatsumōde|Q1032381}} scene, 2015 File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-6.jpg|View from the {{jalink|worship hall|Q3125745}} toward the {{jalink|Noh stage}} File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-2.jpg|{{jalink|yin-yang stones}} ({{jalink|male|Q8441}}) File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-3.jpg|Yin-yang stones ({{jalink|female|Q467}}) === References === *''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' (Report on Shikinaisha Surveys), Vol. 22, San'yōdō, Kōgakkan University Press, Shōwa 55 === Access === *By car: Approximately 5 minutes from {{jalink|Miki-Higashi IC|Q10866141}} on the {{jalink|San'yō Expressway|Q7413195}}. === Surroundings === *{{jalink|Misaka Siphon Bridge|Q11488870}} *{{jalink|Shijimi Stone Chamber|Q11491045}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commons|Misaka-jinja (Miki)}} *[https://misaka-jinja.com/ Misaka Shrine] (official shrine website) *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6310024.html Misaka Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Agency) *[http://www.kita-harima.jp/modules/xdirectory/singlelink.php?cid=110&lid=220 Misaka Shrine] (Miki City Tourism Association) *[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/video/bura1708.htm Shijimi, the land of Fudoki] (Miki City Internet Video Station "Burari to Miki e") {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みさかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Misaka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Misaka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:三木市の建築物]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{Otheruses|兵庫県三木市の神社|周防国の式内社|三坂神社 (山口市)}} {{神社 |名称 = 御坂神社 |画像 = [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-3.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地 = {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}{{jalink|Miki|Q31238}}, Shijimi-chō Misaka 243 |位置 = {{Coord|34|47|29.1|N|135|03|23.9|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinai Shōsha|Q134917287}}; former {{jalink|district shrine|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Three-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}}, copper-plate roofing |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = May 3 |神事 = Oyumi-sai (February 11); {{jalink|Onda-sai}} (May 5) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御坂神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Misaka Shrine''' (みさかじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} located at Shijimi-chō Misaka 243, {{jalink|Miki|Q31238}}, {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}. It is listed in the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'' as "{{jalink|Harima Province|Q1047523}}, {{jalink|Minō District|Q6868901|Minō (Minō) District}}, one seat, Misaka Shrine," making it a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine ranking}} was {{jalink|district shrine|Q134917316}}. It is the {{jalink|ujigami|Q767655}} of Shijimi. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-5.jpg|thumb|right|260px|View of the main hall from the worship hall]] The principal enshrined deity is '''Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami''' (やとかけすみもろのかみ), with {{jalink|Ōmononushi}} and {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ashiharashio no Kami}} enshrined alongside. All three are alternative names for {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944}}[https://misaka-jinja.com/gosaijin/ Misaka Shrine official website - Enshrined deities of Misaka Shrine]. === History === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-Honden.jpg|thumb|right|260px|Worship hall and main hall]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Nou Butai.jpg|thumb|right|260px|{{jalink|Noh stage}}]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-1.jpg|thumb|right|260px|The grove of Misaka Shrine, where the yin-yang stones are enshrined.]] The date of establishment is unknown. The ''{{jalink|Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726}}'' records: "The deity Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami, residing at Misaka in the village of Shijimi, descended from heaven to the peak of Misaka after Ōmononushi and Ashiharashio had finished pacifying the land." It is also said that the 17th Emperor, {{jalink|Emperor Richū|Q329704}}, visited the shrine. On that occasion, Emperor Richū reportedly said, "The flow of this river is exceedingly beautiful," and from this the entire region came to be called "Minō District." Furthermore, because a {{jalink|freshwater clam|Q1796597}} crawled up onto the Emperor's dining table, the area came to be known as Shijimi (志染)[https://misaka-jinja.com/yuisyo/ Misaka Shrine official website - History of Misaka Shrine] . During the {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058|Tenshō era}}, when {{jalink|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|Hashiba Hideyoshi}} was conducting his campaign against the western provinces, he laid siege to {{jalink|Miki Castle|Q11242473|Kamayama}}, the stronghold of {{jalink|Bessho Nagaharu|Q837435}} in Miki. The fires of war from that time (the {{jalink|Tenshō Disturbance}}) destroyed all the ancient records, making the details unclear. It is said that the shrine was subsequently relocated to its present site in {{jalink|Keichō|Q1328185}} 13 ({{jalink|1608|Q6758}}). In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), the shrine was ranked as a district shrine, and in {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 3 ({{jalink|1914|Q2083}}) April, it was designated as a {{jalink|shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and cloth}}. Within Miki City, there are a total of eight shrines called "'''Misaka'''," including this Misaka Shrine, as well as '''Misaka Shrine''' (三坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (美坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (御酒神社), and others. === Precincts === The main hall and the worship hall, which is structurally connected to it, face a {{jalink|Noh stage}} and a shrine office in front. The area behind the main hall is a grove where {{jalink|yin-yang stones}} are enshrined[https://misaka-jinja.com/gallery/ Misaka Shrine official website - Photo]. === Festival calendar [https://misaka-jinja.com/event/ Misaka Shrine official website - Annual events schedule]=== *{{jalink|January 1|Q2150}} {{jalink|Saitan-sai|Q11546824}} *{{jalink|February 11|Q2335}} Oyumi-sai (Archery Festival). Previously held on the first Day of the Boar after {{jalink|Lunar New Year|Q1944136}}, it was changed to February 11, {{jalink|National Foundation Day|Q12981823}}, from {{jalink|Shōwa 55}}. Six archers each shoot 2 {{jalink|arrow|Q45922}}s per round over 5 rounds, for a total of 10 arrows aimed at a {{jalink|target|Q11269217}} approximately 20 meters away. If an arrow hits the center of the target, it foretells a {{jalink|bountiful harvest}} for that year. If it hits the area marked "oni" (demon) sealed on the back of the target, it is called "[[Wikt:破邪|haja]] kenshō" (breaking evil and revealing righteousness), meaning that evil is defeated and correct principles are made manifest in the world. Originally, the archers would perform {{jalink|misogi|Q1936265}} in the {{jalink|Shijimi River|Q11491046}} beside the shrine, then silently receive {{jalink|sacred sake|Q3313722}} in the "Nagatoko no Gi" ceremony at the {{jalink|worship hall|Q3125745}}, but this is now omitted. In addition, the "Urayasu no Mai" dance is performed by a {{jalink|miko|Q1047139}}, {{jalink|Sarutahiko}} performs the "Tenchi-barai, Shihō-barai" (purification of heaven and earth, purification of the four directions), and finally {{jalink|mochi-maki|Q11666809}} (mochi-throwing) is held. *{{jalink|February 11|Q2335}} Kinen-sai (Prayer Festival) *May 4-5, Annual Festival. Two {{jalink|festival float|Q2039612}}s are dedicated. *Sunday following the May annual festival: {{jalink|Onda-sai}} *{{jalink|June 30|Q2657}} {{jalink|Ōharae|Q11261910|Ōharae-sai}} *{{jalink|November 23|Q3021}} {{jalink|Niiname-sai|Q11501518}} *{{jalink|December 28|Q2832}} Ōharae-sai === Gallery === File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-1.jpg|{{jalink|hatsumōde|Q1032381}} scene, 2015 File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-6.jpg|View from the {{jalink|worship hall|Q3125745}} toward the {{jalink|Noh stage}} File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-2.jpg|{{jalink|yin-yang stones}} ({{jalink|male|Q8441}}) File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-3.jpg|Yin-yang stones ({{jalink|female|Q467}}) === References === *''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' (Report on Shikinaisha Surveys), Vol. 22, San'yōdō, Kōgakkan University Press, Shōwa 55 === Access === *By car: Approximately 5 minutes from {{jalink|Miki-Higashi IC|Q10866141}} on the {{jalink|San'yō Expressway|Q7413195}}. === Surroundings === *{{jalink|Misaka Siphon Bridge|Q11488870}} *{{jalink|Shijimi Stone Chamber|Q11491045}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commons|Misaka-jinja (Miki)}} *[https://misaka-jinja.com/ Misaka Shrine] (official shrine website) *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6310024.html Misaka Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Agency) *[http://www.kita-harima.jp/modules/xdirectory/singlelink.php?cid=110&lid=220 Misaka Shrine] (Miki City Tourism Association) *[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/video/bura1708.htm Shijimi, the land of Fudoki] (Miki City Internet Video Station "Burari to Miki e") {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みさかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Misaka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Misaka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:三木市の建築物]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

Worship hall |所在地 = {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}{{jalink|Miki|Q31238}}, Shijimi-chō Misaka 243 |位置 = {{Coord|34|47|29.1|N|135|03|23.9|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinai Shōsha|Q134917287}}; former {{jalink|district shrine|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Three-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}}, copper-plate roofing |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = May 3 |神事 = Oyumi-sai (February 11); {{jalink|Onda-sai}} (May 5) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御坂神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Misaka Shrine''' (みさかじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} located at Shijimi-chō Misaka 243, {{jalink|Miki|Q31238}}, {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}. It is listed in the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'' as "{{jalink|Harima Province|Q1047523}}, {{jalink|Minō District|Q6868901|Minō (Minō) District}}, one seat, Misaka Shrine," making it a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine ranking}} was {{jalink|district shrine|Q134917316}}. It is the {{jalink|ujigami|Q767655}} of Shijimi. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-5.jpg|thumb|right|260px|View of the main hall from the worship hall]] The principal enshrined deity is '''Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami''' (やとかけすみもろのかみ), with {{jalink|Ōmononushi}} and {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ashiharashio no Kami}} enshrined alongside. All three are alternative names for {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944}}[https://misaka-jinja.com/gosaijin/ Misaka Shrine official website - Enshrined deities of Misaka Shrine]. === History === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-Honden.jpg|thumb|right|260px|Worship hall and main hall]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Nou Butai.jpg|thumb|right|260px|{{jalink|Noh stage}}]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-1.jpg|thumb|right|260px|The grove of Misaka Shrine, where the yin-yang stones are enshrined.]] The date of establishment is unknown. The ''{{jalink|Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726}}'' records: "The deity Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami, residing at Misaka in the village of Shijimi, descended from heaven to the peak of Misaka after Ōmononushi and Ashiharashio had finished pacifying the land." It is also said that the 17th Emperor, {{jalink|Emperor Richū|Q329704}}, visited the shrine. On that occasion, Emperor Richū reportedly said, "The flow of this river is exceedingly beautiful," and from this the entire region came to be called "Minō District." Furthermore, because a {{jalink|freshwater clam|Q1796597}} crawled up onto the Emperor's dining table, the area came to be known as Shijimi (志染)[https://misaka-jinja.com/yuisyo/ Misaka Shrine official website - History of Misaka Shrine] . During the {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058|Tenshō era}}, when {{jalink|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|Hashiba Hideyoshi}} was conducting his campaign against the western provinces, he laid siege to {{jalink|Miki Castle|Q11242473|Kamayama}}, the stronghold of {{jalink|Bessho Nagaharu|Q837435}} in Miki. The fires of war from that time (the {{jalink|Tenshō Disturbance}}) destroyed all the ancient records, making the details unclear. It is said that the shrine was subsequently relocated to its present site in {{jalink|Keichō|Q1328185}} 13 ({{jalink|1608|Q6758}}). In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), the shrine was ranked as a district shrine, and in {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 3 ({{jalink|1914|Q2083}}) April, it was designated as a {{jalink|shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and cloth}}. Within Miki City, there are a total of eight shrines called "'''Misaka'''," including this Misaka Shrine, as well as '''Misaka Shrine''' (三坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (美坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (御酒神社), and others. === Precincts === The main hall and the worship hall, which is structurally connected to it, face a {{jalink|Noh stage}} and a shrine office in front. The area behind the main hall is a grove where {{jalink|yin-yang stones}} are enshrined[https://misaka-jinja.com/gallery/ Misaka Shrine official website - Photo]. === Festival calendar [https://misaka-jinja.com/event/ Misaka Shrine official website - Annual events schedule]=== *{{jalink|January 1|Q2150}} {{jalink|Saitan-sai|Q11546824}} *{{jalink|February 11|Q2335}} Oyumi-sai (Archery Festival). Previously held on the first Day of the Boar after {{jalink|Lunar New Year|Q1944136}}, it was changed to February 11, {{jalink|National Foundation Day|Q12981823}}, from {{jalink|Shōwa 55}}. Six archers each shoot 2 {{jalink|arrow|Q45922}}s per round over 5 rounds, for a total of 10 arrows aimed at a {{jalink|target|Q11269217}} approximately 20 meters away. If an arrow hits the center of the target, it foretells a {{jalink|bountiful harvest}} for that year. If it hits the area marked "oni" (demon) sealed on the back of the target, it is called "[[Wikt:破邪|haja]] kenshō" (breaking evil and revealing righteousness), meaning that evil is defeated and correct principles are made manifest in the world. Originally, the archers would perform {{jalink|misogi|Q1936265}} in the {{jalink|Shijimi River|Q11491046}} beside the shrine, then silently receive {{jalink|sacred sake|Q3313722}} in the "Nagatoko no Gi" ceremony at the {{jalink|worship hall|Q3125745}}, but this is now omitted. In addition, the "Urayasu no Mai" dance is performed by a {{jalink|miko|Q1047139}}, {{jalink|Sarutahiko}} performs the "Tenchi-barai, Shihō-barai" (purification of heaven and earth, purification of the four directions), and finally {{jalink|mochi-maki|Q11666809}} (mochi-throwing) is held. *{{jalink|February 11|Q2335}} Kinen-sai (Prayer Festival) *May 4-5, Annual Festival. Two {{jalink|festival float|Q2039612}}s are dedicated. *Sunday following the May annual festival: {{jalink|Onda-sai}} *{{jalink|June 30|Q2657}} {{jalink|Ōharae|Q11261910|Ōharae-sai}} *{{jalink|November 23|Q3021}} {{jalink|Niiname-sai|Q11501518}} *{{jalink|December 28|Q2832}} Ōharae-sai === Gallery === File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-1.jpg|{{jalink|hatsumōde|Q1032381}} scene, 2015 File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-6.jpg|View from the {{jalink|worship hall|Q3125745}} toward the {{jalink|Noh stage}} File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-2.jpg|{{jalink|yin-yang stones}} ({{jalink|male|Q8441}}) File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-3.jpg|Yin-yang stones ({{jalink|female|Q467}}) === References === *''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' (Report on Shikinaisha Surveys), Vol. 22, San'yōdō, Kōgakkan University Press, Shōwa 55 === Access === *By car: Approximately 5 minutes from {{jalink|Miki-Higashi IC|Q10866141}} on the {{jalink|San'yō Expressway|Q7413195}}. === Surroundings === *{{jalink|Misaka Siphon Bridge|Q11488870}} *{{jalink|Shijimi Stone Chamber|Q11491045}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commons|Misaka-jinja (Miki)}} *[https://misaka-jinja.com/ Misaka Shrine] (official shrine website) *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6310024.html Misaka Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Agency) *[http://www.kita-harima.jp/modules/xdirectory/singlelink.php?cid=110&lid=220 Misaka Shrine] (Miki City Tourism Association) *[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/video/bura1708.htm Shijimi, the land of Fudoki] (Miki City Internet Video Station "Burari to Miki e") {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みさかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Misaka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Misaka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:三木市の建築物]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木市の歴史]]

address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県三木市志染町御坂243', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Misaka-jinja (Miki)}} [[JA:御坂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11488875}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|兵庫県三木市の神社|周防国の式内社|三坂神社 (山口市)}} {{神社 |名称 = 御坂神社 |画像 = [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-3.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地 = {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}{{jalink|Miki|Q31238}}, Shijimi-chō Misaka 243 |位置 = {{Coord|34|47|29.1|N|135|03|23.9|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinai Shōsha|Q134917287}}; former {{jalink|district shrine|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Three-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}}, copper-plate roofing |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = May 3 |神事 = Oyumi-sai (February 11); {{jalink|Onda-sai}} (May 5) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御坂神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Misaka Shrine''' (みさかじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} located at Shijimi-chō Misaka 243, {{jalink|Miki|Q31238}}, {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}. It is listed in the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'' as "{{jalink|Harima Province|Q1047523}}, {{jalink|Minō District|Q6868901|Minō (Minō) District}}, one seat, Misaka Shrine," making it a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine ranking}} was {{jalink|district shrine|Q134917316}}. It is the {{jalink|ujigami|Q767655}} of Shijimi. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-5.jpg|thumb|right|260px|View of the main hall from the worship hall]] The principal enshrined deity is '''Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami''' (やとかけすみもろのかみ), with {{jalink|Ōmononushi}} and {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ashiharashio no Kami}} enshrined alongside. All three are alternative names for {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944}}[https://misaka-jinja.com/gosaijin/ Misaka Shrine official website - Enshrined deities of Misaka Shrine]. === History === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-Honden.jpg|thumb|right|260px|Worship hall and main hall]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Nou Butai.jpg|thumb|right|260px|{{jalink|Noh stage}}]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-1.jpg|thumb|right|260px|The grove of Misaka Shrine, where the yin-yang stones are enshrined.]] The date of establishment is unknown. The ''{{jalink|Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726}}'' records: "The deity Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami, residing at Misaka in the village of Shijimi, descended from heaven to the peak of Misaka after Ōmononushi and Ashiharashio had finished pacifying the land." It is also said that the 17th Emperor, {{jalink|Emperor Richū|Q329704}}, visited the shrine. On that occasion, Emperor Richū reportedly said, "The flow of this river is exceedingly beautiful," and from this the entire region came to be called "Minō District." Furthermore, because a {{jalink|freshwater clam|Q1796597}} crawled up onto the Emperor's dining table, the area came to be known as Shijimi (志染)[https://misaka-jinja.com/yuisyo/ Misaka Shrine official website - History of Misaka Shrine] . During the {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058|Tenshō era}}, when {{jalink|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|Hashiba Hideyoshi}} was conducting his campaign against the western provinces, he laid siege to {{jalink|Miki Castle|Q11242473|Kamayama}}, the stronghold of {{jalink|Bessho Nagaharu|Q837435}} in Miki. The fires of war from that time (the {{jalink|Tenshō Disturbance}}) destroyed all the ancient records, making the details unclear. It is said that the shrine was subsequently relocated to its present site in {{jalink|Keichō|Q1328185}} 13 ({{jalink|1608|Q6758}}). In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), the shrine was ranked as a district shrine, and in {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 3 ({{jalink|1914|Q2083}}) April, it was designated as a {{jalink|shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and cloth}}. Within Miki City, there are a total of eight shrines called "'''Misaka'''," including this Misaka Shrine, as well as '''Misaka Shrine''' (三坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (美坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (御酒神社), and others. === Precincts === The main hall and the worship hall, which is structurally connected to it, face a {{jalink|Noh stage}} and a shrine office in front. The area behind the main hall is a grove where {{jalink|yin-yang stones}} are enshrined[https://misaka-jinja.com/gallery/ Misaka Shrine official website - Photo]. === Festival calendar [https://misaka-jinja.com/event/ Misaka Shrine official website - Annual events schedule]=== *{{jalink|January 1|Q2150}} {{jalink|Saitan-sai|Q11546824}} *{{jalink|February 11|Q2335}} Oyumi-sai (Archery Festival). Previously held on the first Day of the Boar after {{jalink|Lunar New Year|Q1944136}}, it was changed to February 11, {{jalink|National Foundation Day|Q12981823}}, from {{jalink|Shōwa 55}}. Six archers each shoot 2 {{jalink|arrow|Q45922}}s per round over 5 rounds, for a total of 10 arrows aimed at a {{jalink|target|Q11269217}} approximately 20 meters away. If an arrow hits the center of the target, it foretells a {{jalink|bountiful harvest}} for that year. If it hits the area marked "oni" (demon) sealed on the back of the target, it is called "[[Wikt:破邪|haja]] kenshō" (breaking evil and revealing righteousness), meaning that evil is defeated and correct principles are made manifest in the world. Originally, the archers would perform {{jalink|misogi|Q1936265}} in the {{jalink|Shijimi River|Q11491046}} beside the shrine, then silently receive {{jalink|sacred sake|Q3313722}} in the "Nagatoko no Gi" ceremony at the {{jalink|worship hall|Q3125745}}, but this is now omitted. In addition, the "Urayasu no Mai" dance is performed by a {{jalink|miko|Q1047139}}, {{jalink|Sarutahiko}} performs the "Tenchi-barai, Shihō-barai" (purification of heaven and earth, purification of the four directions), and finally {{jalink|mochi-maki|Q11666809}} (mochi-throwing) is held. *{{jalink|February 11|Q2335}} Kinen-sai (Prayer Festival) *May 4-5, Annual Festival. Two {{jalink|festival float|Q2039612}}s are dedicated. *Sunday following the May annual festival: {{jalink|Onda-sai}} *{{jalink|June 30|Q2657}} {{jalink|Ōharae|Q11261910|Ōharae-sai}} *{{jalink|November 23|Q3021}} {{jalink|Niiname-sai|Q11501518}} *{{jalink|December 28|Q2832}} Ōharae-sai === Gallery === File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-1.jpg|{{jalink|hatsumōde|Q1032381}} scene, 2015 File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-6.jpg|View from the {{jalink|worship hall|Q3125745}} toward the {{jalink|Noh stage}} File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-2.jpg|{{jalink|yin-yang stones}} ({{jalink|male|Q8441}}) File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-3.jpg|Yin-yang stones ({{jalink|female|Q467}}) === References === *''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' (Report on Shikinaisha Surveys), Vol. 22, San'yōdō, Kōgakkan University Press, Shōwa 55 === Access === *By car: Approximately 5 minutes from {{jalink|Miki-Higashi IC|Q10866141}} on the {{jalink|San'yō Expressway|Q7413195}}. === Surroundings === *{{jalink|Misaka Siphon Bridge|Q11488870}} *{{jalink|Shijimi Stone Chamber|Q11491045}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commons|Misaka-jinja (Miki)}} *[https://misaka-jinja.com/ Misaka Shrine] (official shrine website) *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6310024.html Misaka Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Agency) *[http://www.kita-harima.jp/modules/xdirectory/singlelink.php?cid=110&lid=220 Misaka Shrine] (Miki City Tourism Association) *[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/video/bura1708.htm Shijimi, the land of Fudoki] (Miki City Internet Video Station "Burari to Miki e") {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みさかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Misaka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Misaka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:三木市の建築物]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]
