It is located in [[Harima Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická {{Otheruses|兵庫県三木市の神社|周防国の式内社|三坂神社 (山口市)}} {{神社 |名称 = 御坂神社 |画像 = [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-3.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Miki|Q31238|WD=Q31238}}, Shijimi-chō Misaka 243 |位置 = {{Coord|34|47|29.1|N|135|03|23.9|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami |社格 = {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}}; former {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}, copper-plate roofing |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = May 3 |神事 = Oyumi-sai (February 11); {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Onda-sai|UNKNOWN}} (May 5) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御坂神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Misaka Shrine''' (みさかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located at Shijimi-chō Misaka 243, {{ill|lt=Miki|Q31238|WD=Q31238}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}. It is listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' as "{{ill|lt=Harima Province|Q1047523|WD=Q1047523}}, {{ill|lt=Minō District|Q6868901|WD=Q6868901|lt=Minō (Minō) District}}, one seat, Misaka Shrine," making it a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}. Its former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine ranking|UNKNOWN}} was {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655}} of Shijimi. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-5.jpg|thumb|right|260px|View of the main hall from the worship hall]] The principal enshrined deity is '''Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami''' (やとかけすみもろのかみ), with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmononushi|UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ashiharashio no Kami}} enshrined alongside. All three are alternative names for {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944}}[https://misaka-jinja.com/gosaijin/ Misaka Shrine official website - Enshrined deities of Misaka Shrine]. === History === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-Honden.jpg|thumb|right|260px|Worship hall and main hall]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Nou Butai.jpg|thumb|right|260px|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noh stage|UNKNOWN}}]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-1.jpg|thumb|right|260px|The grove of Misaka Shrine, where the yin-yang stones are enshrined.]] The date of establishment is unknown. The ''{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726}}'' records: "The deity Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami, residing at Misaka in the village of Shijimi, descended from heaven to the peak of Misaka after Ōmononushi and Ashiharashio had finished pacifying the land." It is also said that the 17th Emperor, {{ill|lt=Emperor Richū|Q329704|WD=Q329704}}, visited the shrine. On that occasion, Emperor Richū reportedly said, "The flow of this river is exceedingly beautiful," and from this the entire region came to be called "Minō District." Furthermore, because a {{ill|lt=freshwater clam|Q1796597|WD=Q1796597}} crawled up onto the Emperor's dining table, the area came to be known as Shijimi (志染)[https://misaka-jinja.com/yuisyo/ Misaka Shrine official website - History of Misaka Shrine] . During the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō era}}, when {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|lt=Hashiba Hideyoshi}} was conducting his campaign against the western provinces, he laid siege to {{ill|lt=Miki Castle|Q11242473|WD=Q11242473|lt=Kamayama}}, the stronghold of {{ill|lt=Bessho Nagaharu|Q837435|WD=Q837435}} in Miki. The fires of war from that time (the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenshō Disturbance|UNKNOWN}}) destroyed all the ancient records, making the details unclear. It is said that the shrine was subsequently relocated to its present site in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1608|Q6758|WD=Q6758}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), the shrine was ranked as a district shrine, and in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083}}) April, it was designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and cloth|UNKNOWN}}. Within Miki City, there are a total of eight shrines called "'''Misaka'''," including this Misaka Shrine, as well as '''Misaka Shrine''' (三坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (美坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (御酒神社), and others. === Precincts === The main hall and the worship hall, which is structurally connected to it, face a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noh stage|UNKNOWN}} and a shrine office in front. The area behind the main hall is a grove where {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=yin-yang stones|UNKNOWN}} are enshrined[https://misaka-jinja.com/gallery/ Misaka Shrine official website - Photo]. === Festival calendar [https://misaka-jinja.com/event/ Misaka Shrine official website - Annual events schedule]=== *{{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150}} {{ill|lt=Saitan-sai|Q11546824|WD=Q11546824}} *{{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335}} Oyumi-sai (Archery Festival). Previously held on the first Day of the Boar after {{ill|lt=Lunar New Year|Q1944136|WD=Q1944136}}, it was changed to February 11, {{ill|lt=National Foundation Day|Q12981823|WD=Q12981823}}, from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shōwa 55|UNKNOWN}}. Six archers each shoot 2 {{ill|lt=arrow|Q45922|WD=Q45922}}s per round over 5 rounds, for a total of 10 arrows aimed at a {{ill|lt=target|Q11269217|WD=Q11269217}} approximately 20 meters away. If an arrow hits the center of the target, it foretells a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=bountiful harvest|UNKNOWN}} for that year. If it hits the area marked "oni" (demon) sealed on the back of the target, it is called "[[Wikt:破邪|haja]] kenshō" (breaking evil and revealing righteousness), meaning that evil is defeated and correct principles are made manifest in the world. Originally, the archers would perform {{ill|lt=misogi|Q1936265|WD=Q1936265}} in the {{ill|lt=Shijimi River|Q11491046|WD=Q11491046}} beside the shrine, then silently receive {{ill|lt=sacred sake|Q3313722|WD=Q3313722}} in the "Nagatoko no Gi" ceremony at the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745}}, but this is now omitted. In addition, the "Urayasu no Mai" dance is performed by a {{ill|lt=miko|Q1047139|WD=Q1047139}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko|UNKNOWN}} performs the "Tenchi-barai, Shihō-barai" (purification of heaven and earth, purification of the four directions), and finally {{ill|lt=mochi-maki|Q11666809|WD=Q11666809}} (mochi-throwing) is held. *{{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335}} Kinen-sai (Prayer Festival) *May 4-5, Annual Festival. Two {{ill|lt=festival float|Q2039612|WD=Q2039612}}s are dedicated. *Sunday following the May annual festival: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Onda-sai|UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657}} {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910|lt=Ōharae-sai}} *{{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021}} {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518}} *{{ill|lt=December 28|Q2832|WD=Q2832}} Ōharae-sai === Gallery === File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-1.jpg|{{ill|lt=hatsumōde|Q1032381|WD=Q1032381}} scene, 2015 File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-6.jpg|View from the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745}} toward the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noh stage|UNKNOWN}} File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-2.jpg|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=yin-yang stones|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=male|Q8441|WD=Q8441}}) File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-3.jpg|Yin-yang stones ({{ill|lt=female|Q467|WD=Q467}}) === References === *''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' (Report on Shikinaisha Surveys), Vol. 22, San'yōdō, Kōgakkan University Press, Shōwa 55 === Access === *By car: Approximately 5 minutes from {{ill|lt=Miki-Higashi IC|Q10866141|WD=Q10866141}} on the {{ill|lt=San'yō Expressway|Q7413195|WD=Q7413195}}. === Surroundings === *{{ill|lt=Misaka Siphon Bridge|Q11488870|WD=Q11488870}} *{{ill|lt=Shijimi Stone Chamber|Q11491045|WD=Q11491045}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commons|Misaka-jinja (Miki)}} *[https://misaka-jinja.com/ It is listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' as "{{ill|lt=Harima Province|Q1047523|WD=Q1047523}}, {{ill|lt=Minō District|Q6868901|WD=Q6868901|lt=Minō (Minō) District}}, one seat, Misaka Shrine," making it a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. Its former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine ranking|1=UNKNOWN}} was {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655}} of Shijimi. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-5.jpg|thumb|right|260px|View of the main hall from the worship hall]] The principal enshrined deity is '''Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami''' (やとかけすみもろのかみ), with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmononushi|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ashiharashio no Kami}} enshrined alongside. All three are alternative names for {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944}}[https://misaka-jinja.com/gosaijin/ Misaka Shrine official website - Enshrined deities of Misaka Shrine]. === History === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-Honden.jpg|thumb|right|260px|Worship hall and main hall]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Nou Butai.jpg|thumb|right|260px|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noh stage|1=UNKNOWN}}]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-1.jpg|thumb|right|260px|The grove of Misaka Shrine, where the yin-yang stones are enshrined.]] The date of establishment is unknown. The ''{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726}}'' records: "The deity Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami, residing at Misaka in the village of Shijimi, descended from heaven to the peak of Misaka after Ōmononushi and Ashiharashio had finished pacifying the land." It is also said that the 17th Emperor, {{ill|lt=Emperor Richū|Q329704|WD=Q329704}}, visited the shrine. On that occasion, Emperor Richū reportedly said, "The flow of this river is exceedingly beautiful," and from this the entire region came to be called "Minō District." Furthermore, because a {{ill|lt=freshwater clam|Q1796597|WD=Q1796597}} crawled up onto the Emperor's dining table, the area came to be known as Shijimi (志染)[https://misaka-jinja.com/yuisyo/ Misaka Shrine official website - History of Misaka Shrine] . During the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō era}}, when {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|lt=Hashiba Hideyoshi}} was conducting his campaign against the western provinces, he laid siege to {{ill|lt=Miki Castle|Q11242473|WD=Q11242473|lt=Kamayama}}, the stronghold of {{ill|lt=Bessho Nagaharu|Q837435|WD=Q837435}} in Miki. The fires of war from that time (the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenshō Disturbance|1=UNKNOWN}}) destroyed all the ancient records, making the details unclear. It is said that the shrine was subsequently relocated to its present site in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1608|Q6758|WD=Q6758}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), the shrine was ranked as a district shrine, and in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083}}) April, it was designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and cloth|1=UNKNOWN}}. Within Miki City, there are a total of eight shrines called "'''Misaka'''," including this Misaka Shrine, as well as '''Misaka Shrine''' (三坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (美坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (御酒神社), and others. === Precincts === The main hall and the worship hall, which is structurally connected to it, face a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noh stage|1=UNKNOWN}} and a shrine office in front. The area behind the main hall is a grove where {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=yin-yang stones|1=UNKNOWN}} are enshrined[https://misaka-jinja.com/gallery/ Misaka Shrine official website - Photo]. === Festival calendar [https://misaka-jinja.com/event/ Misaka Shrine official website - Annual events schedule]=== *{{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150}} {{ill|lt=Saitan-sai|Q11546824|WD=Q11546824}} *{{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335}} Oyumi-sai (Archery Festival). Previously held on the first Day of the Boar after {{ill|lt=Lunar New Year|Q1944136|WD=Q1944136}}, it was changed to February 11, {{ill|lt=National Foundation Day|Q12981823|WD=Q12981823}}, from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shōwa 55|1=UNKNOWN}}. Six archers each shoot 2 {{ill|lt=arrow|Q45922|WD=Q45922}}s per round over 5 rounds, for a total of 10 arrows aimed at a {{ill|lt=target|Q11269217|WD=Q11269217}} approximately 20 meters away. If an arrow hits the center of the target, it foretells a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=bountiful harvest|1=UNKNOWN}} for that year. If it hits the area marked "oni" (demon) sealed on the back of the target, it is called "[[Wikt:破邪|haja]] kenshō" (breaking evil and revealing righteousness), meaning that evil is defeated and correct principles are made manifest in the world. Originally, the archers would perform {{ill|lt=misogi|Q1936265|WD=Q1936265}} in the {{ill|lt=Shijimi River|Q11491046|WD=Q11491046}} beside the shrine, then silently receive {{ill|lt=sacred sake|Q3313722|WD=Q3313722}} in the "Nagatoko no Gi" ceremony at the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745}}, but this is now omitted. In addition, the "Urayasu no Mai" dance is performed by a {{ill|lt=miko|Q1047139|WD=Q1047139}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko|1=UNKNOWN}} performs the "Tenchi-barai, Shihō-barai" (purification of heaven and earth, purification of the four directions), and finally {{ill|lt=mochi-maki|Q11666809|WD=Q11666809}} (mochi-throwing) is held. *{{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335}} Kinen-sai (Prayer Festival) *May 4-5, Annual Festival. Two {{ill|lt=festival float|Q2039612|WD=Q2039612}}s are dedicated. *Sunday following the May annual festival: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Onda-sai|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657}} {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910|lt=Ōharae-sai}} *{{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021}} {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518}} *{{ill|lt=December 28|Q2832|WD=Q2832}} Ōharae-sai === Gallery === File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-1.jpg|{{ill|lt=hatsumōde|Q1032381|WD=Q1032381}} scene, 2015 File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-6.jpg|View from the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745}} toward the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noh stage|1=UNKNOWN}} File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-2.jpg|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=yin-yang stones|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=male|Q8441|WD=Q8441}}) File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-3.jpg|Yin-yang stones ({{ill|lt=female|Q467|WD=Q467}}) === References === *''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' (Report on Shikinaisha Surveys), Vol. 22, San'yōdō, Kōgakkan University Press, Shōwa 55 === Access === *By car: Approximately 5 minutes from {{ill|lt=Miki-Higashi IC|Q10866141|WD=Q10866141}} on the {{ill|lt=San'yō Expressway|Q7413195|WD=Q7413195}}. === Surroundings === *{{ill|lt=Misaka Siphon Bridge|Q11488870|WD=Q11488870}} *{{ill|lt=Shijimi Stone Chamber|Q11491045|WD=Q11491045}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commons|Misaka-jinja (Miki)}} *[https://misaka-jinja.com/ Fudoki] (Miki City Internet Video Station "Burari to Miki e") {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みさかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Misaka Worship hall |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Miki|Q31238|WD=Q31238}}, Shijimi-chō Misaka 243 |位置 = {{Coord|34|47|29.1|N|135|03|23.9|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami |社格 = {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}}; former {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}, copper-plate roofing |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = May 3 |神事 = Oyumi-sai (February 11); {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Onda-sai|1=UNKNOWN}} (May 5) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御坂神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Misaka Shrine''' (みさかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located at Shijimi-chō Misaka 243, {{ill|lt=Miki|Q31238|WD=Q31238}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}. It is listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' as "{{ill|lt=Harima Province|Q1047523|WD=Q1047523}}, {{ill|lt=Minō District|Q6868901|WD=Q6868901|lt=Minō (Minō) District}}, one seat, Misaka Shrine," making it a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine ranking|1=UNKNOWN}} was {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655}} of Shijimi. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-5.jpg|thumb|right|260px|View of the main hall from the worship hall]] The principal enshrined deity is '''Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami''' (やとかけすみもろのかみ), with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmononushi|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ashiharashio no Kami}} enshrined alongside. All three are alternative names for {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944}}[https://misaka-jinja.com/gosaijin/ Misaka Shrine official website - Enshrined deities of Misaka Shrine]. === History === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-Honden.jpg|thumb|right|260px|Worship hall and main hall]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Nou Butai.jpg|thumb|right|260px|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noh stage|1=UNKNOWN}}]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-1.jpg|thumb|right|260px|The grove of Misaka Shrine, where the yin-yang stones are enshrined.]] The date of establishment is unknown. The ''{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726}}'' records: "The deity Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami, residing at Misaka in the village of Shijimi, descended from heaven to the peak of Misaka after Ōmononushi and Ashiharashio had finished pacifying the land." It is also said that the 17th Emperor, {{ill|lt=Emperor Richū|Q329704|WD=Q329704}}, visited the shrine. On that occasion, Emperor Richū reportedly said, "The flow of this river is exceedingly beautiful," and from this the entire region came to be called "Minō District." Furthermore, because a {{ill|lt=freshwater clam|Q1796597|WD=Q1796597}} crawled up onto the Emperor's dining table, the area came to be known as Shijimi (志染)[https://misaka-jinja.com/yuisyo/ Misaka Shrine official website - History of Misaka Shrine] . During the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō era}}, when {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|lt=Hashiba Hideyoshi}} was conducting his campaign against the western provinces, he laid siege to {{ill|lt=Miki Castle|Q11242473|WD=Q11242473|lt=Kamayama}}, the stronghold of {{ill|lt=Bessho Nagaharu|Q837435|WD=Q837435}} in Miki. The fires of war from that time (the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenshō Disturbance|1=UNKNOWN}}) destroyed all the ancient records, making the details unclear. It is said that the shrine was subsequently relocated to its present site in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1608|Q6758|WD=Q6758}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), the shrine was ranked as a district shrine, and in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083}}) April, it was designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and cloth|1=UNKNOWN}}. Within Miki City, there are a total of eight shrines called "'''Misaka'''," including this Misaka Shrine, as well as '''Misaka Shrine''' (三坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (美坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (御酒神社), and others. === Precincts === The main hall and the worship hall, which is structurally connected to it, face a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noh stage|1=UNKNOWN}} and a shrine office in front. The area behind the main hall is a grove where {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=yin-yang stones|1=UNKNOWN}} are enshrined[https://misaka-jinja.com/gallery/ Misaka Shrine official website - Photo]. === Festival calendar [https://misaka-jinja.com/event/ Misaka Shrine official website - Annual events schedule]=== *{{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150}} {{ill|lt=Saitan-sai|Q11546824|WD=Q11546824}} *{{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335}} Oyumi-sai (Archery Festival). Previously held on the first Day of the Boar after {{ill|lt=Lunar New Year|Q1944136|WD=Q1944136}}, it was changed to February 11, {{ill|lt=National Foundation Day|Q12981823|WD=Q12981823}}, from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shōwa 55|1=UNKNOWN}}. Six archers each shoot 2 {{ill|lt=arrow|Q45922|WD=Q45922}}s per round over 5 rounds, for a total of 10 arrows aimed at a {{ill|lt=target|Q11269217|WD=Q11269217}} approximately 20 meters away. If an arrow hits the center of the target, it foretells a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=bountiful harvest|1=UNKNOWN}} for that year. If it hits the area marked "oni" (demon) sealed on the back of the target, it is called "[[Wikt:破邪|haja]] kenshō" (breaking evil and revealing righteousness), meaning that evil is defeated and correct principles are made manifest in the world. Originally, the archers would perform {{ill|lt=misogi|Q1936265|WD=Q1936265}} in the {{ill|lt=Shijimi River|Q11491046|WD=Q11491046}} beside the shrine, then silently receive {{ill|lt=sacred sake|Q3313722|WD=Q3313722}} in the "Nagatoko no Gi" ceremony at the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745}}, but this is now omitted. In addition, the "Urayasu no Mai" dance is performed by a {{ill|lt=miko|Q1047139|WD=Q1047139}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko|1=UNKNOWN}} performs the "Tenchi-barai, Shihō-barai" (purification of heaven and earth, purification of the four directions), and finally {{ill|lt=mochi-maki|Q11666809|WD=Q11666809}} (mochi-throwing) is held. *{{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335}} Kinen-sai (Prayer Festival) *May 4-5, Annual Festival. Two {{ill|lt=festival float|Q2039612|WD=Q2039612}}s are dedicated. *Sunday following the May annual festival: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Onda-sai|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657}} {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910|lt=Ōharae-sai}} *{{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021}} {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518}} *{{ill|lt=December 28|Q2832|WD=Q2832}} Ōharae-sai === {{Otheruses|兵庫県三木市の神社|周防国の式内社|三坂神社 (山口市)}} {{神社 |名称 = 御坂神社 |画像 = [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-3.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Miki|Q31238|WD=Q31238}}, Shijimi-chō Misaka 243 |位置 = {{Coord|34|47|29.1|N|135|03|23.9|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami |社格 = {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}}; former {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}, copper-plate roofing |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = May 3 |神事 = Oyumi-sai (February 11); {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Onda-sai|1=UNKNOWN}} (May 5) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御坂神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Misaka Shrine''' (みさかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located at Shijimi-chō Misaka 243, {{ill|lt=Miki|Q31238|WD=Q31238}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}. It is listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' as "{{ill|lt=Harima Province|Q1047523|WD=Q1047523}}, {{ill|lt=Minō District|Q6868901|WD=Q6868901|lt=Minō (Minō) District}}, one seat, Misaka Shrine," making it a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine ranking|1=UNKNOWN}} was {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655}} of Shijimi. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-5.jpg|thumb|right|260px|View of the main hall from the worship hall]] The principal enshrined deity is '''Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami''' (やとかけすみもろのかみ), with {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ashiharashio no Kami}} enshrined alongside. All three are alternative names for {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944}}[https://misaka-jinja.com/gosaijin/ Misaka Shrine official website - Enshrined deities of Misaka Shrine]. === History === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-Honden.jpg|thumb|right|260px|Worship hall and main hall]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Nou Butai.jpg|thumb|right|260px|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noh stage|1=UNKNOWN}}]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-1.jpg|thumb|right|260px|The grove of Misaka Shrine, where the yin-yang stones are enshrined.]] The date of establishment is unknown. The ''{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726}}'' records: "The deity Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami, residing at Misaka in the village of Shijimi, descended from heaven to the peak of Misaka after Ōmononushi and Ashiharashio had finished pacifying the land." It is also said that the 17th Emperor, {{ill|lt=Emperor Richū|Q329704|WD=Q329704}}, visited the shrine. On that occasion, Emperor Richū reportedly said, "The flow of this river is exceedingly beautiful," and from this the entire region came to be called "Minō District." Furthermore, because a {{ill|lt=freshwater clam|Q1796597|WD=Q1796597}} crawled up onto the Emperor's dining table, the area came to be known as Shijimi (志染)[https://misaka-jinja.com/yuisyo/ Misaka Shrine official website - History of Misaka Shrine] . During the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō era}}, when {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|lt=Hashiba Hideyoshi}} was conducting his campaign against the western provinces, he laid siege to {{ill|lt=Miki Castle|Q11242473|WD=Q11242473|lt=Kamayama}}, the stronghold of {{ill|lt=Bessho Nagaharu|Q837435|WD=Q837435}} in Miki. The fires of war from that time (the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenshō Disturbance|1=UNKNOWN}}) destroyed all the ancient records, making the details unclear. It is said that the shrine was subsequently relocated to its present site in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1608|Q6758|WD=Q6758}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), the shrine was ranked as a district shrine, and in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083}}) April, it was designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and cloth|1=UNKNOWN}}. Within Miki City, there are a total of eight shrines called "'''Misaka'''," including this Misaka Shrine, as well as '''Misaka Shrine''' (三坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (美坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (御酒神社), and others. === Precincts === The main hall and the worship hall, which is structurally connected to it, face a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noh stage|1=UNKNOWN}} and a shrine office in front. The area behind the main hall is a grove where {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=yin-yang stones|1=UNKNOWN}} are enshrined[https://misaka-jinja.com/gallery/ Misaka Shrine official website - Photo]. === Festival calendar [https://misaka-jinja.com/event/ Misaka Shrine official website - Annual events schedule]=== *{{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150}} {{ill|lt=Saitan-sai|Q11546824|WD=Q11546824}} *{{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335}} Oyumi-sai (Archery Festival). Previously held on the first Day of the Boar after {{ill|lt=Lunar New Year|Q1944136|WD=Q1944136}}, it was changed to February 11, {{ill|lt=National Foundation Day|Q12981823|WD=Q12981823}}, from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shōwa 55|1=UNKNOWN}}. Six archers each shoot 2 {{ill|lt=arrow|Q45922|WD=Q45922}}s per round over 5 rounds, for a total of 10 arrows aimed at a {{ill|lt=target|Q11269217|WD=Q11269217}} approximately 20 meters away. If an arrow hits the center of the target, it foretells a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=bountiful harvest|1=UNKNOWN}} for that year. If it hits the area marked "oni" (demon) sealed on the back of the target, it is called "[[Wikt:破邪|haja]] kenshō" (breaking evil and revealing righteousness), meaning that evil is defeated and correct principles are made manifest in the world. Originally, the archers would perform {{ill|lt=misogi|Q1936265|WD=Q1936265}} in the {{ill|lt=Shijimi River|Q11491046|WD=Q11491046}} beside the shrine, then silently receive {{ill|lt=sacred sake|Q3313722|WD=Q3313722}} in the "Nagatoko no Gi" ceremony at the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745}}, but this is now omitted. In addition, the "Urayasu no Mai" dance is performed by a {{ill|lt=miko|Q1047139|WD=Q1047139}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko|1=UNKNOWN}} performs the "Tenchi-barai, Shihō-barai" (purification of heaven and earth, purification of the four directions), and finally {{ill|lt=mochi-maki|Q11666809|WD=Q11666809}} (mochi-throwing) is held. *{{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335}} Kinen-sai (Prayer Festival) *May 4-5, Annual Festival. Two {{ill|lt=festival float|Q2039612|WD=Q2039612}}s are dedicated. *Sunday following the May annual festival: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Onda-sai|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657}} {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910|lt=Ōharae-sai}} *{{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021}} {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518}} *{{ill|lt=December 28|Q2832|WD=Q2832}} Ōharae-sai === Gallery === File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-1.jpg|{{ill|lt=hatsumōde|Q1032381|WD=Q1032381}} scene, 2015 File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-6.jpg|View from the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745}} toward the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noh stage|1=UNKNOWN}} File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-2.jpg|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=yin-yang stones|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=male|Q8441|WD=Q8441}}) File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-3.jpg|Yin-yang stones ({{ill|lt=female|Q467|WD=Q467}}) === References === *''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' (Report on Shikinaisha Surveys), Vol. 22, San'yōdō, Kōgakkan University Press, Shōwa 55 === Access === *By car: Approximately 5 minutes from {{ill|lt=Miki-Higashi IC|Q10866141|WD=Q10866141}} on the {{ill|lt=San'yō Expressway|Q7413195|WD=Q7413195}}. === Surroundings === *{{ill|lt=Misaka Siphon Bridge|Q11488870|WD=Q11488870}} *{{ill|lt=Shijimi Stone Chamber|Q11491045|WD=Q11491045}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commons|Misaka-jinja (Miki)}} *[https://misaka-jinja.com/ Misaka Shrine] (official shrine website) *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6310024.html Misaka Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Agency) *[http://www.kita-harima.jp/modules/xdirectory/singlelink.php?cid=110&lid=220 Misaka Shrine] (Miki City Tourism Association) *[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/video/bura1708.htm Shijimi, the land of Fudoki] (Miki City Internet Video Station "Burari to Miki e") {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みさかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Misaka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Misaka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:三木市の建築物]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Worship hall |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Miki|Q31238|WD=Q31238}}, Shijimi-chō Misaka 243 |位置 = {{Coord|34|47|29.1|N|135|03|23.9|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami |社格 = {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}}; former {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}, copper-plate roofing |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = May 3 |神事 = Oyumi-sai (February 11); {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Onda-sai|1=UNKNOWN}} (May 5) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御坂神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Misaka Shrine''' (みさかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located at Shijimi-chō Misaka 243, {{ill|lt=Miki|Q31238|WD=Q31238}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}. It is listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' as "{{ill|lt=Harima Province|Q1047523|WD=Q1047523}}, {{ill|lt=Minō District|Q6868901|WD=Q6868901|lt=Minō (Minō) District}}, one seat, Misaka Shrine," making it a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine ranking|1=UNKNOWN}} was {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655}} of Shijimi. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-5.jpg|thumb|right|260px|View of the main hall from the worship hall]] The principal enshrined deity is '''Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami''' (やとかけすみもろのかみ), with {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ashiharashio no Kami}} enshrined alongside. All three are alternative names for {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944}}[https://misaka-jinja.com/gosaijin/ Misaka Shrine official website - Enshrined deities of Misaka Shrine]. === History === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-Honden.jpg|thumb|right|260px|Worship hall and main hall]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Nou Butai.jpg|thumb|right|260px|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noh stage|1=UNKNOWN}}]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-1.jpg|thumb|right|260px|The grove of Misaka Shrine, where the yin-yang stones are enshrined.]] The date of establishment is unknown. The ''{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726}}'' records: "The deity Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami, residing at Misaka in the village of Shijimi, descended from heaven to the peak of Misaka after Ōmononushi and Ashiharashio had finished pacifying the land." It is also said that the 17th Emperor, {{ill|lt=Emperor Richū|Q329704|WD=Q329704}}, visited the shrine. On that occasion, Emperor Richū reportedly said, "The flow of this river is exceedingly beautiful," and from this the entire region came to be called "Minō District." Furthermore, because a {{ill|lt=freshwater clam|Q1796597|WD=Q1796597}} crawled up onto the Emperor's dining table, the area came to be known as Shijimi (志染)[https://misaka-jinja.com/yuisyo/ Misaka Shrine official website - History of Misaka Shrine] . During the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō era}}, when {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|lt=Hashiba Hideyoshi}} was conducting his campaign against the western provinces, he laid siege to {{ill|lt=Miki Castle|Q11242473|WD=Q11242473|lt=Kamayama}}, the stronghold of {{ill|lt=Bessho Nagaharu|Q837435|WD=Q837435}} in Miki. The fires of war from that time (the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenshō Disturbance|1=UNKNOWN}}) destroyed all the ancient records, making the details unclear. It is said that the shrine was subsequently relocated to its present site in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1608|Q6758|WD=Q6758}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), the shrine was ranked as a district shrine, and in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083}}) April, it was designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and cloth|1=UNKNOWN}}. Within Miki City, there are a total of eight shrines called "'''Misaka'''," including this Misaka Shrine, as well as '''Misaka Shrine''' (三坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (美坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (御酒神社), and others. === Precincts === The main hall and the worship hall, which is structurally connected to it, face a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noh stage|1=UNKNOWN}} and a shrine office in front. The area behind the main hall is a grove where {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=yin-yang stones|1=UNKNOWN}} are enshrined[https://misaka-jinja.com/gallery/ Misaka Shrine official website - Photo]. === Festival calendar [https://misaka-jinja.com/event/ Misaka Shrine official website - Annual events schedule]=== *{{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150}} {{ill|lt=Saitan-sai|Q11546824|WD=Q11546824}} *{{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335}} Oyumi-sai (Archery Festival). Previously held on the first Day of the Boar after {{ill|lt=Lunar New Year|Q1944136|WD=Q1944136}}, it was changed to February 11, {{ill|lt=National Foundation Day|Q12981823|WD=Q12981823}}, from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shōwa 55|1=UNKNOWN}}. Six archers each shoot 2 {{ill|lt=arrow|Q45922|WD=Q45922}}s per round over 5 rounds, for a total of 10 arrows aimed at a {{ill|lt=target|Q11269217|WD=Q11269217}} approximately 20 meters away. If an arrow hits the center of the target, it foretells a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=bountiful harvest|1=UNKNOWN}} for that year. If it hits the area marked "oni" (demon) sealed on the back of the target, it is called "[[Wikt:破邪|haja]] kenshō" (breaking evil and revealing righteousness), meaning that evil is defeated and correct principles are made manifest in the world. Originally, the archers would perform {{ill|lt=misogi|Q1936265|WD=Q1936265}} in the {{ill|lt=Shijimi River|Q11491046|WD=Q11491046}} beside the shrine, then silently receive {{ill|lt=sacred sake|Q3313722|WD=Q3313722}} in the "Nagatoko no Gi" ceremony at the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745}}, but this is now omitted. In addition, the "Urayasu no Mai" dance is performed by a {{ill|lt=miko|Q1047139|WD=Q1047139}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko|1=UNKNOWN}} performs the "Tenchi-barai, Shihō-barai" (purification of heaven and earth, purification of the four directions), and finally {{ill|lt=mochi-maki|Q11666809|WD=Q11666809}} (mochi-throwing) is held. *{{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335}} Kinen-sai (Prayer Festival) *May 4-5, Annual Festival. Two {{ill|lt=festival float|Q2039612|WD=Q2039612}}s are dedicated. *Sunday following the May annual festival: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Onda-sai|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657}} {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910|lt=Ōharae-sai}} *{{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021}} {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518}} *{{ill|lt=December 28|Q2832|WD=Q2832}} Ōharae-sai === Gallery === File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-1.jpg|{{ill|lt=hatsumōde|Q1032381|WD=Q1032381}} scene, 2015 File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-6.jpg|View from the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745}} toward the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noh stage|1=UNKNOWN}} File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-2.jpg|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=yin-yang stones|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=male|Q8441|WD=Q8441}}) File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-3.jpg|Yin-yang stones ({{ill|lt=female|Q467|WD=Q467}}) === References === *''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' (Report on Shikinaisha Surveys), Vol. 22, San'yōdō, Kōgakkan University Press, Shōwa 55 === Access === *By car: Approximately 5 minutes from {{ill|lt=Miki-Higashi IC|Q10866141|WD=Q10866141}} on the {{ill|lt=San'yō Expressway|Q7413195|WD=Q7413195}}. === Surroundings === *{{ill|lt=Misaka Siphon Bridge|Q11488870|WD=Q11488870}} *{{ill|lt=Shijimi Stone Chamber|Q11491045|WD=Q11491045}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commons|Misaka-jinja (Miki)}} *[https://misaka-jinja.com/ (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県三木市志染町御坂243', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Misaka-jinja (Miki)}} [[JA:御坂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11488875}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|兵庫県三木市の神社|周防国の式内社|三坂神社 (山口市)}} {{神社 |名称 = 御坂神社 |画像 = [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-3.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Miki|Q31238|WD=Q31238|1=Miki, Hyōgo}}, Shijimi-chō Misaka 243 |位置 = {{Coord|34|47|29.1|N|135|03|23.9|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami |社格 = {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}; former {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, copper-plate roofing |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = May 3 |神事 = Oyumi-sai (February 11); {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Onda-sai|1=UNKNOWN}} (May 5) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御坂神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Misaka Shrine''' (みさかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located at Shijimi-chō Misaka 243, {{ill|lt=Miki|Q31238|WD=Q31238|1=Miki, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' as "{{ill|lt=Harima Province|Q1047523|WD=Q1047523|1=Harima Province}}, {{ill|lt=Minō District|Q6868901|WD=Q6868901|lt=Minō (Minō) District|1=Mino District, Hyōgo}}, one seat, Misaka Shrine," making it a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine ranking|1=UNKNOWN}} was {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of Shijimi. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-5.jpg|thumb|right|260px|View of the main hall from the worship hall]] The principal enshrined deity is '''Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami''' (やとかけすみもろのかみ), with {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|1=Ōmononushi}} and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ashiharashio no Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} enshrined alongside. All three are alternative names for {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}[https://misaka-jinja.com/gosaijin/ Misaka Shrine official website - Enshrined deities of Misaka Shrine]. === History === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-Honden.jpg|thumb|right|260px|Worship hall and main hall]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Nou Butai.jpg|thumb|right|260px|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noh stage|1=UNKNOWN}}]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-1.jpg|thumb|right|260px|The grove of Misaka Shrine, where the yin-yang stones are enshrined.]] The date of establishment is unknown. The ''{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726|1=Harima no Kuni Fudoki}}'' records: "The deity Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami, residing at Misaka in the village of Shijimi, descended from heaven to the peak of Misaka after Ōmononushi and Ashiharashio had finished pacifying the land." It is also said that the 17th Emperor, {{ill|lt=Emperor Richū|Q329704|WD=Q329704|1=Emperor Richū}}, visited the shrine. On that occasion, Emperor Richū reportedly said, "The flow of this river is exceedingly beautiful," and from this the entire region came to be called "Minō District." Furthermore, because a {{ill|lt=freshwater clam|Q1796597|WD=Q1796597|1=Corbiculidae}} crawled up onto the Emperor's dining table, the area came to be known as Shijimi (志染)[https://misaka-jinja.com/yuisyo/ Misaka Shrine official website - History of Misaka Shrine] . During the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}}, when {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|lt=Hashiba Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} was conducting his campaign against the western provinces, he laid siege to {{ill|lt=Miki Castle|Q11242473|WD=Q11242473|lt=Kamayama|1=Miki Castle}}, the stronghold of {{ill|lt=Bessho Nagaharu|Q837435|WD=Q837435|1=Bessho Nagaharu}} in Miki. The fires of war from that time (the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenshō Disturbance|1=UNKNOWN}}) destroyed all the ancient records, making the details unclear. It is said that the shrine was subsequently relocated to its present site in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1608|Q6758|WD=Q6758|1=1608}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), the shrine was ranked as a district shrine, and in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083|1=1914}}) April, it was designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and cloth|1=UNKNOWN}}. Within Miki City, there are a total of eight shrines called "'''Misaka'''," including this Misaka Shrine, as well as '''Misaka Shrine''' (三坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (美坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (御酒神社), and others. === Precincts === The main hall and the worship hall, which is structurally connected to it, face a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noh stage|1=UNKNOWN}} and a shrine office in front. The area behind the main hall is a grove where {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=yin-yang stones|1=UNKNOWN}} are enshrined[https://misaka-jinja.com/gallery/ Misaka Shrine official website - Photo]. === Festival calendar [https://misaka-jinja.com/event/ Misaka Shrine official website - Annual events schedule]=== *{{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}} {{ill|lt=Saitan-sai|Q11546824|WD=Q11546824|1=Saitan-sai}} *{{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335|1=February 11}} Oyumi-sai (Archery Festival). Previously held on the first Day of the Boar after {{ill|lt=Lunar New Year|Q1944136|WD=Q1944136|1=Lunar New Year}}, it was changed to February 11, {{ill|lt=National Foundation Day|Q12981823|WD=Q12981823|1=National Foundation Day}}, from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shōwa 55|1=UNKNOWN}}. Six archers each shoot 2 {{ill|lt=arrow|Q45922|WD=Q45922|1=Arrow}}s per round over 5 rounds, for a total of 10 arrows aimed at a {{ill|lt=target|Q11269217|WD=Q11269217|1=target}} approximately 20 meters away. If an arrow hits the center of the target, it foretells a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=bountiful harvest|1=UNKNOWN}} for that year. If it hits the area marked "oni" (demon) sealed on the back of the target, it is called "[[Wikt:破邪|haja]] kenshō" (breaking evil and revealing righteousness), meaning that evil is defeated and correct principles are made manifest in the world. Originally, the archers would perform {{ill|lt=misogi|Q1936265|WD=Q1936265|1=Misogi}} in the {{ill|lt=Shijimi River|Q11491046|WD=Q11491046|1=Shijimi River}} beside the shrine, then silently receive {{ill|lt=sacred sake|Q3313722|WD=Q3313722|1=Omiki}} in the "Nagatoko no Gi" ceremony at the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}, but this is now omitted. In addition, the "Urayasu no Mai" dance is performed by a {{ill|lt=miko|Q1047139|WD=Q1047139|1=Miko}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko|1=UNKNOWN}} performs the "Tenchi-barai, Shihō-barai" (purification of heaven and earth, purification of the four directions), and finally {{ill|lt=mochi-maki|Q11666809|WD=Q11666809|1=mochimaki}} (mochi-throwing) is held. *{{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335|1=February 11}} Kinen-sai (Prayer Festival) *May 4-5, Annual Festival. Two {{ill|lt=festival float|Q2039612|WD=Q2039612|1=Yatai (food cart)}}s are dedicated. *Sunday following the May annual festival: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Onda-sai|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}} {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910|lt=Ōharae-sai|1=Ōharae-shiki}} *{{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}} {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} *{{ill|lt=December 28|Q2832|WD=Q2832|1=December 28}} Ōharae-sai === Gallery === File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-1.jpg|{{ill|lt=hatsumōde|Q1032381|WD=Q1032381|1=Hatsumōde}} scene, 2015 File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-6.jpg|View from the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}} toward the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noh stage|1=UNKNOWN}} File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-2.jpg|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=yin-yang stones|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=male|Q8441|WD=Q8441|1=Man}}) File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-3.jpg|Yin-yang stones ({{ill|lt=female|Q467|WD=Q467|1=Woman}}) === References === *''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' (Report on Shikinaisha Surveys), Vol. 22, San'yōdō, Kōgakkan University Press, Shōwa 55 === Access === *By car: Approximately 5 minutes from {{ill|lt=Miki-Higashi IC|Q10866141|WD=Q10866141|1=Miki-higashi interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=San'yō Expressway|Q7413195|WD=Q7413195|1=San'yō Expressway}}. === Surroundings === *{{ill|lt=Misaka Siphon Bridge|Q11488870|WD=Q11488870|1=Misaka Siphon Bridge}} *{{ill|lt=Shijimi Stone Chamber|Q11491045|WD=Q11491045|1=Shijimi-no-iwamuro}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commons|Misaka-jinja (Miki)}} *[https://misaka-jinja.com/ Misaka Shrine] (official shrine website) *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6310024.html Misaka Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Agency) *[http://www.kita-harima.jp/modules/xdirectory/singlelink.php?cid=110&lid=220 Misaka Shrine] (Miki City Tourism Association) *[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/video/bura1708.htm Shijimi, the land of Fudoki] (Miki City Internet Video Station "Burari to Miki e") {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県三木市志染町御坂243', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Misaka-jinja (Miki)}} [[JA:御坂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11488875}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|兵庫県三木市の神社|周防国の式内社|三坂神社 (山口市)}} {{神社 |名称 = 御坂神社 |画像 = [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-3.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Miki|Q31238|WD=Q31238|1=Miki, Hyōgo}}, Shijimi-chō Misaka 243 |位置 = {{Coord|34|47|29.1|N|135|03|23.9|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami |社格 = {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}; former {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, copper-plate roofing |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = May 3 |神事 = Oyumi-sai (February 11); {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Onda-sai|1=UNKNOWN}} (May 5) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御坂神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Misaka Shrine''' (みさかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located at Shijimi-chō Misaka 243, {{ill|lt=Miki|Q31238|WD=Q31238|1=Miki, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' as "{{ill|lt=Harima Province|Q1047523|WD=Q1047523|1=Harima Province}}, {{ill|lt=Minō District|Q6868901|WD=Q6868901|lt=Minō (Minō) District|1=Mino District, Hyōgo}}, one seat, Misaka Shrine," making it a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine ranking|1=UNKNOWN}} was {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of Shijimi. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-5.jpg|thumb|right|260px|View of the main hall from the worship hall]] The principal enshrined deity is '''Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami''' (やとかけすみもろのかみ), with {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|1=Ōmononushi}} and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ashiharashio no Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} enshrined alongside. All three are alternative names for {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}[https://misaka-jinja.com/gosaijin/ Misaka Shrine official website - Enshrined deities of Misaka Shrine]. === History === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-Honden.jpg|thumb|right|260px|Worship hall and main hall]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Nou Butai.jpg|thumb|right|260px|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noh stage|1=UNKNOWN}}]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-1.jpg|thumb|right|260px|The grove of Misaka Shrine, where the yin-yang stones are enshrined.]] The date of establishment is unknown. The ''{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726|1=Harima no Kuni Fudoki}}'' records: "The deity Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami, residing at Misaka in the village of Shijimi, descended from heaven to the peak of Misaka after Ōmononushi and Ashiharashio had finished pacifying the land." It is also said that the 17th Emperor, {{ill|lt=Emperor Richū|Q329704|WD=Q329704|1=Emperor Richū}}, visited the shrine. On that occasion, Emperor Richū reportedly said, "The flow of this river is exceedingly beautiful," and from this the entire region came to be called "Minō District." Furthermore, because a {{ill|lt=freshwater clam|Q1796597|WD=Q1796597|1=Corbiculidae}} crawled up onto the Emperor's dining table, the area came to be known as Shijimi (志染)[https://misaka-jinja.com/yuisyo/ Misaka Shrine official website - History of Misaka Shrine] . During the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}}, when {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|lt=Hashiba Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} was conducting his campaign against the western provinces, he laid siege to {{ill|lt=Miki Castle|Q11242473|WD=Q11242473|lt=Kamayama|1=Miki Castle}}, the stronghold of {{ill|lt=Bessho Nagaharu|Q837435|WD=Q837435|1=Bessho Nagaharu}} in Miki. The fires of war from that time (the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenshō Disturbance|1=UNKNOWN}}) destroyed all the ancient records, making the details unclear. It is said that the shrine was subsequently relocated to its present site in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1608|Q6758|WD=Q6758|1=1608}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), the shrine was ranked as a district shrine, and in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083|1=1914}}) April, it was designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and cloth|1=UNKNOWN}}. Within Miki City, there are a total of eight shrines called "'''Misaka'''," including this Misaka Shrine, as well as '''Misaka Shrine''' (三坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (美坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (御酒神社), and others. === Precincts === The main hall and the worship hall, which is structurally connected to it, face a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noh stage|1=UNKNOWN}} and a shrine office in front. The area behind the main hall is a grove where {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=yin-yang stones|1=UNKNOWN}} are enshrined[https://misaka-jinja.com/gallery/ Misaka Shrine official website - Photo]. === Festival calendar [https://misaka-jinja.com/event/ Misaka Shrine official website - Annual events schedule]=== *{{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}} {{ill|lt=Saitan-sai|Q11546824|WD=Q11546824|1=Saitan-sai}} *{{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335|1=February 11}} Oyumi-sai (Archery Festival). Previously held on the first Day of the Boar after {{ill|lt=Lunar New Year|Q1944136|WD=Q1944136|1=Lunar New Year}}, it was changed to February 11, {{ill|lt=National Foundation Day|Q12981823|WD=Q12981823|1=National Foundation Day}}, from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shōwa 55|1=UNKNOWN}}. Six archers each shoot 2 {{ill|lt=arrow|Q45922|WD=Q45922|1=Arrow}}s per round over 5 rounds, for a total of 10 arrows aimed at a {{ill|lt=target|Q11269217|WD=Q11269217|1=target}} approximately 20 meters away. If an arrow hits the center of the target, it foretells a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=bountiful harvest|1=UNKNOWN}} for that year. If it hits the area marked "oni" (demon) sealed on the back of the target, it is called "[[Wikt:破邪|haja]] kenshō" (breaking evil and revealing righteousness), meaning that evil is defeated and correct principles are made manifest in the world. Originally, the archers would perform {{ill|lt=misogi|Q1936265|WD=Q1936265|1=Misogi}} in the {{ill|lt=Shijimi River|Q11491046|WD=Q11491046|1=Shijimi River}} beside the shrine, then silently receive {{ill|lt=sacred sake|Q3313722|WD=Q3313722|1=Omiki}} in the "Nagatoko no Gi" ceremony at the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}, but this is now omitted. In addition, the "Urayasu no Mai" dance is performed by a {{ill|lt=miko|Q1047139|WD=Q1047139|1=Miko}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko|1=UNKNOWN}} performs the "Tenchi-barai, Shihō-barai" (purification of heaven and earth, purification of the four directions), and finally {{ill|lt=mochi-maki|Q11666809|WD=Q11666809|1=mochimaki}} (mochi-throwing) is held. *{{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335|1=February 11}} Kinen-sai (Prayer Festival) *May 4-5, Annual Festival. Two {{ill|lt=festival float|Q2039612|WD=Q2039612|1=Yatai (food cart)}}s are dedicated. *Sunday following the May annual festival: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Onda-sai|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}} {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910|lt=Ōharae-sai|1=Ōharae-shiki}} *{{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}} {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} *{{ill|lt=December 28|Q2832|WD=Q2832|1=December 28}} Ōharae-sai === Gallery === File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-1.jpg|{{ill|lt=hatsumōde|Q1032381|WD=Q1032381|1=Hatsumōde}} scene, 2015 File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-6.jpg|View from the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}} toward the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noh stage|1=UNKNOWN}} File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-2.jpg|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=yin-yang stones|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=male|Q8441|WD=Q8441|1=Man}}) File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-3.jpg|Yin-yang stones ({{ill|lt=female|Q467|WD=Q467|1=Woman}}) === References === *''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' (Report on Shikinaisha Surveys), Vol. 22, San'yōdō, Kōgakkan University Press, Shōwa 55 === Access === *By car: Approximately 5 minutes from {{ill|lt=Miki-Higashi IC|Q10866141|WD=Q10866141|1=Miki-higashi interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=San'yō Expressway|Q7413195|WD=Q7413195|1=San'yō Expressway}}. === Surroundings === *{{ill|lt=Misaka Siphon Bridge|Q11488870|WD=Q11488870|1=Misaka Siphon Bridge}} *{{ill|lt=Shijimi Stone Chamber|Q11491045|WD=Q11491045|1=Shijimi-no-iwamuro}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commons|Misaka-jinja (Miki)}} *[https://misaka-jinja.com/ Misaka Shrine] (official shrine website) *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6310024.html Misaka Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Agency) *[http://www.kita-harima.jp/modules/xdirectory/singlelink.php?cid=110&lid=220 Misaka Shrine] (Miki City Tourism Association) *[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/video/bura1708.htm Shijimi, the land of Fudoki] (Miki City Internet Video Station "Burari to Miki e") {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} Its former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine ranking|1=UNKNOWN}} was {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of Shijimi. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-5.jpg|thumb|right|260px|View of the main hall from the worship hall]] The principal enshrined deity is '''Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami''' (やとかけすみもろのかみ), with {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|1=Ōmononushi}} and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ashiharashio no Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} enshrined alongside. All three are alternative names for {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}[https://misaka-jinja.com/gosaijin/ Misaka Shrine official website - Enshrined deities of Misaka Shrine]. === History === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-Honden.jpg|thumb|right|260px|Worship hall and main hall]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Nou Butai.jpg|thumb|right|260px|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noh stage|1=UNKNOWN}}]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-1.jpg|thumb|right|260px|The grove of Misaka Shrine, where the yin-yang stones are enshrined.]] The date of establishment is unknown. The ''{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726|1=Harima no Kuni Fudoki}}'' records: "The deity Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami, residing at Misaka in the village of Shijimi, descended from heaven to the peak of Misaka after Ōmononushi and Ashiharashio had finished pacifying the land." It is also said that the 17th Emperor, {{ill|lt=Emperor Richū|Q329704|WD=Q329704|1=Emperor Richū}}, visited the shrine. On that occasion, Emperor Richū reportedly said, "The flow of this river is exceedingly beautiful," and from this the entire region came to be called "Minō District." Furthermore, because a {{ill|lt=freshwater clam|Q1796597|WD=Q1796597|1=Corbiculidae}} crawled up onto the Emperor's dining table, the area came to be known as Shijimi (志染)[https://misaka-jinja.com/yuisyo/ Misaka Shrine official website - History of Misaka Shrine] . During the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}}, when {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|lt=Hashiba Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} was conducting his campaign against the western provinces, he laid siege to {{ill|lt=Miki Castle|Q11242473|WD=Q11242473|lt=Kamayama|1=Miki Castle}}, the stronghold of {{ill|lt=Bessho Nagaharu|Q837435|WD=Q837435|1=Bessho Nagaharu}} in Miki. The fires of war from that time (the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenshō Disturbance|1=UNKNOWN}}) destroyed all the ancient records, making the details unclear. It is said that the shrine was subsequently relocated to its present site in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1608|Q6758|WD=Q6758|1=1608}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), the shrine was ranked as a district shrine, and in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083|1=1914}}) April, it was designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and cloth|1=UNKNOWN}}. Within Miki City, there are a total of eight shrines called "'''Misaka'''," including this Misaka Shrine, as well as '''Misaka Shrine''' (三坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (美坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (御酒神社), and others. === Precincts === The main hall and the worship hall, which is structurally connected to it, face a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noh stage|1=UNKNOWN}} and a shrine office in front. The area behind the main hall is a grove where {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=yin-yang stones|1=UNKNOWN}} are enshrined[https://misaka-jinja.com/gallery/ Misaka Shrine official website - Photo]. === Festival calendar [https://misaka-jinja.com/event/ Misaka Shrine official website - Annual events schedule]=== *{{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}} {{ill|lt=Saitan-sai|Q11546824|WD=Q11546824|1=Saitan-sai}} *{{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335|1=February 11}} Oyumi-sai (Archery Festival). Previously held on the first Day of the Boar after {{ill|lt=Lunar New Year|Q1944136|WD=Q1944136|1=Lunar New Year}}, it was changed to February 11, {{ill|lt=National Foundation Day|Q12981823|WD=Q12981823|1=National Foundation Day}}, from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shōwa 55|1=UNKNOWN}}. Six archers each shoot 2 {{ill|lt=arrow|Q45922|WD=Q45922|1=Arrow}}s per round over 5 rounds, for a total of 10 arrows aimed at a {{ill|lt=target|Q11269217|WD=Q11269217|1=target}} approximately 20 meters away. If an arrow hits the center of the target, it foretells a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=bountiful harvest|1=UNKNOWN}} for that year. If it hits the area marked "oni" (demon) sealed on the back of the target, it is called "[[Wikt:破邪|haja]] kenshō" (breaking evil and revealing righteousness), meaning that evil is defeated and correct principles are made manifest in the world. Originally, the archers would perform {{ill|lt=misogi|Q1936265|WD=Q1936265|1=Misogi}} in the {{ill|lt=Shijimi River|Q11491046|WD=Q11491046|1=Shijimi River}} beside the shrine, then silently receive {{ill|lt=sacred sake|Q3313722|WD=Q3313722|1=Omiki}} in the "Nagatoko no Gi" ceremony at the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}, but this is now omitted. In addition, the "Urayasu no Mai" dance is performed by a {{ill|lt=miko|Q1047139|WD=Q1047139|1=Miko}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko|1=UNKNOWN}} performs the "Tenchi-barai, Shihō-barai" (purification of heaven and earth, purification of the four directions), and finally {{ill|lt=mochi-maki|Q11666809|WD=Q11666809|1=mochimaki}} (mochi-throwing) is held. *{{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335|1=February 11}} Kinen-sai (Prayer Festival) *May 4-5, Annual Festival. Two {{ill|lt=festival float|Q2039612|WD=Q2039612|1=Yatai (food cart)}}s are dedicated. *Sunday following the May annual festival: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Onda-sai|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}} {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910|lt=Ōharae-sai|1=Ōharae-shiki}} *{{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}} {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} *{{ill|lt=December 28|Q2832|WD=Q2832|1=December 28}} Ōharae-sai === Gallery === File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-1.jpg|{{ill|lt=hatsumōde|Q1032381|WD=Q1032381|1=Hatsumōde}} scene, 2015 File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-6.jpg|View from the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}} toward the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noh stage|1=UNKNOWN}} File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-2.jpg|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=yin-yang stones|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=male|Q8441|WD=Q8441|1=Man}}) File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-3.jpg|Yin-yang stones ({{ill|lt=female|Q467|WD=Q467|1=Woman}}) === References === *''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' (Report on Shikinaisha Surveys), Vol. 22, San'yōdō, Kōgakkan University Press, Shōwa 55 === Access === *By car: Approximately 5 minutes from {{ill|lt=Miki-Higashi IC|Q10866141|WD=Q10866141|1=Miki-higashi interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=San'yō Expressway|Q7413195|WD=Q7413195|1=San'yō Expressway}}. === Surroundings === *{{ill|lt=Misaka Siphon Bridge|Q11488870|WD=Q11488870|1=Misaka Siphon Bridge}} *{{ill|lt=Shijimi Stone Chamber|Q11491045|WD=Q11491045|1=Shijimi-no-iwamuro}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commons|Misaka-jinja (Miki)}} *[https://misaka-jinja.com/ Misaka Shrine] (official shrine website) *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6310024.html Misaka Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Agency) *[http://www.kita-harima.jp/modules/xdirectory/singlelink.php?cid=110&lid=220 Misaka Shrine] (Miki City Tourism Association) *[http://www.city.miki.lg.jp/video/bura1708.htm Shijimi, the land of Fudoki] (Miki City Internet Video Station "Burari to Miki e") {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Otheruses|兵庫県三木市の神社|周防国の式内社|三坂神社 (山口市)}} {{神社 |名称 = 御坂神社 |画像 = [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-3.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Miki|Q31238|WD=Q31238|1=Miki, Hyōgo}}, Shijimi-chō Misaka 243 |位置 = {{Coord|34|47|29.1|N|135|03|23.9|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami |社格 = {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}; former {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, copper-plate roofing |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = May 3 |神事 = Oyumi-sai (February 11); {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Onda-sai|1=UNKNOWN}} (May 5) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御坂神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Misaka Shrine''' (みさかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located at Shijimi-chō Misaka 243, {{ill|lt=Miki|Q31238|WD=Q31238|1=Miki, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' as "{{ill|lt=Harima Province|Q1047523|WD=Q1047523|1=Harima Province}}, {{ill|lt=Minō District|Q6868901|WD=Q6868901|lt=Minō (Minō) District|1=Mino District, Hyōgo}}, one seat, Misaka Shrine," making it a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine ranking|1=UNKNOWN}} was {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of Shijimi. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-5.jpg|thumb|right|260px|View of the main hall from the worship hall]] The principal enshrined deity is '''Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami''' (やとかけすみもろのかみ), with {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|1=Ōmononushi}} and {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ashiharashio no Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} enshrined alongside. All three are alternative names for {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}[https://misaka-jinja.com/gosaijin/ Misaka Shrine official website - Enshrined deities of Misaka Shrine]. === History === [[File:Misaka-jinja-Haiden-Honden.jpg|thumb|right|260px|Worship hall and main hall]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Nou Butai.jpg|thumb|right|260px|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noh stage|1=UNKNOWN}}]] [[File:Misaka-jinja-Mori-1.jpg|thumb|right|260px|The grove of Misaka Shrine, where the yin-yang stones are enshrined.]] The date of establishment is unknown. The ''{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726|1=Harima no Kuni Fudoki}}'' records: "The deity Yatokakesu Mimoro no Kami, residing at Misaka in the village of Shijimi, descended from heaven to the peak of Misaka after Ōmononushi and Ashiharashio had finished pacifying the land." It is also said that the 17th Emperor, {{ill|lt=Emperor Richū|Q329704|WD=Q329704|1=Emperor Richū}}, visited the shrine. On that occasion, Emperor Richū reportedly said, "The flow of this river is exceedingly beautiful," and from this the entire region came to be called "Minō District." Furthermore, because a {{ill|lt=freshwater clam|Q1796597|WD=Q1796597|1=Corbiculidae}} crawled up onto the Emperor's dining table, the area came to be known as Shijimi (志染)[https://misaka-jinja.com/yuisyo/ Misaka Shrine official website - History of Misaka Shrine] . During the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}}, when {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|lt=Hashiba Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} was conducting his campaign against the western provinces, he laid siege to {{ill|lt=Miki Castle|Q11242473|WD=Q11242473|lt=Kamayama|1=Miki Castle}}, the stronghold of {{ill|lt=Bessho Nagaharu|Q837435|WD=Q837435|1=Bessho Nagaharu}} in Miki. The fires of war from that time (the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenshō Disturbance|1=UNKNOWN}}) destroyed all the ancient records, making the details unclear. It is said that the shrine was subsequently relocated to its present site in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1608|Q6758|WD=Q6758|1=1608}}). In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), the shrine was ranked as a district shrine, and in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083|1=1914}}) April, it was designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and cloth|1=UNKNOWN}}. Within Miki City, there are a total of eight shrines called "'''Misaka'''," including this Misaka Shrine, as well as '''Misaka Shrine''' (三坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (美坂神社), '''Misaka Shrine''' (御酒神社), and others. === Precincts === The main hall and the worship hall, which is structurally connected to it, face a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noh stage|1=UNKNOWN}} and a shrine office in front. The area behind the main hall is a grove where {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=yin-yang stones|1=UNKNOWN}} are enshrined[https://misaka-jinja.com/gallery/ Misaka Shrine official website - Photo]. === Festival calendar [https://misaka-jinja.com/event/ Misaka Shrine official website - Annual events schedule]=== *{{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}} {{ill|lt=Saitan-sai|Q11546824|WD=Q11546824|1=Saitan-sai}} *{{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335|1=February 11}} Oyumi-sai (Archery Festival). Previously held on the first Day of the Boar after {{ill|lt=Lunar New Year|Q1944136|WD=Q1944136|1=Lunar New Year}}, it was changed to February 11, {{ill|lt=National Foundation Day|Q12981823|WD=Q12981823|1=National Foundation Day}}, from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shōwa 55|1=UNKNOWN}}. If an arrow hits the center of the target, it foretells a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=bountiful harvest|1=UNKNOWN}} for that year. If it hits the area marked "oni" (demon) sealed on the back of the target, it is called "[[Wikt:破邪|haja]] kenshō" (breaking evil and revealing righteousness), meaning that evil is defeated and correct principles are made manifest in the world. Originally, the archers would perform {{ill|lt=misogi|Q1936265|WD=Q1936265|1=Misogi}} in the {{ill|lt=Shijimi River|Q11491046|WD=Q11491046|1=Shijimi River}} beside the shrine, then silently receive {{ill|lt=sacred sake|Q3313722|WD=Q3313722|1=Omiki}} in the "Nagatoko no Gi" ceremony at the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}, but this is now omitted. In addition, the "Urayasu no Mai" dance is performed by a {{ill|lt=miko|Q1047139|WD=Q1047139|1=Miko}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko|1=UNKNOWN}} performs the "Tenchi-barai, Shihō-barai" (purification of heaven and earth, purification of the four directions), and finally {{ill|lt=mochi-maki|Q11666809|WD=Q11666809|1=mochimaki}} (mochi-throwing) is held. *{{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335|1=February 11}} Kinen-sai (Prayer Festival) *May 4-5, Annual Festival. Two {{ill|lt=festival float|Q2039612|WD=Q2039612|1=Yatai (food cart)}}s are dedicated. *Sunday following the May annual festival: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Onda-sai|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}} {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910|lt=Ōharae-sai|1=Ōharae-shiki}} *{{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}}
